html:
[422]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 略无仪检 - No decorum or restraint; describes the lack of proper behavior.
  • 挥鞭骤马以为谐谑 - Wave the whip and gallop the horse as a form of jest; describes playful but inappropriate behavior.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦国 - The Prince of Qin, a member of the imperial family.
  • 虢国 - Princess Guo, a member of the imperial family.
  • 韩国 - Princess Han, a member of the imperial family.
  • 国忠 - Consort Guo, a powerful figure in the court.
  • 十宅诸王 - The ten princes, likely referring to the sons of the emperor.
  • 华清宫 - The Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial retreat known for its hot springs and luxurious accommodations.
  • 贵妃 - The concubine, likely Yang Guifei, one of the most famous concubines in Chinese history.
  • 小部音声 - A specialized group of musicians within the imperial music bureau.
  • 长生殿 - The Hall of Eternal Life, a specific location within the palace.
  • 荔 枝香 - A new song composed for the occasion, named after the lychees brought from the south.
  • 左右 - Courtiers or attendants who followed the emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 秦国 - The Prince of Qin.
  • 虢国 - Princess Guo.
  • 韩国 - Princess Han.
  • 国忠 - Consort Guo.
  • 十宅诸王 - The ten princes.
  • 华清宫 - The Huaqing Palace.
  • 贵妃 - The concubine, likely Yang Guifei.
  • 小部音声 - The small department of musicians.
  • 长生殿 - The Hall of Eternal Life.
  • 荔 枝香 - The Fragrance of Lychees.
  • 左右 - Courtiers or attendants.

[423]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其年十一月 - Formal expression meaning 'in the eleventh month of that year', used to specify a particular time.
  • 反幽陵 - Means 'rebel in Youzhou', referring to a region in northern China.
  • 禄山本名轧草山 - Direct statement of Li Yan's (An Lushan's) original name, which is unusual and requires careful handling.
  • 杂种胡人也 - Derogatory term meaning 'a mixed breed of barbarian', reflecting the ethnic prejudice of the time.
  • 母本巫师 - Means 'his mother was a witch', providing background information about his family.
  • 垂肚过膝 - Vivid description of his large belly, which was said to hang below his knees.
  • 自称得三百五十斤 - Means 'he claimed to weigh three hundred and fifty pounds', emphasizing his enormous size.
  • 于上前胡旋舞 - Refers to performing the Hu Xuan Dance in front of the emperor, a dance that involves rapid spinning.
  • 疾如风焉 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'as fast as the wind', describing the speed of the dance.
  • 上尝于勤政楼东间设大金鸡障 - Detailed description of a scene where the emperor set up a large gold chicken screen in the eastern room of the Qinzhen Tower.
  • 令禄山坐 - Means 'ordered Li Yan to sit', indicating a special honor.
  • 其下设百戏 - Means 'below, various entertainments were set up', referring to a variety of performances.
  • 与禄山看焉 - Means 'to watch with Li Yan', indicating that the emperor watched the performances with him.
  • 肃宗谏曰 - Means 'Emperor Suzong advised, saying', introducing a formal speech.
  • 历观今古 - Means 'throughout history', referring to a broad historical perspective.
  • 未闻臣下与君上同坐阅戏 - Means 'I have never heard of ministers sitting with the emperor to watch performances', expressing a concern about propriety.
  • 上私曰 - Means 'the emperor privately said', indicating a private conversation.
  • 渠有异相 - Means 'he has an unusual appearance', referring to Li Yan's physical features.
  • 我禳之故耳 - Means 'it's because I'm warding it off', suggesting a superstitious belief.
  • 又尝与夜宴 - Means 'he also once attended a night banquet', describing another event.
  • 禄山醉卧，化为一猪而龙首 - Fantastical description of Li Yan transforming into a pig with a dragon head while drunk.
  • 左右遽告帝 - Means 'the attendants hurriedly informed the emperor', indicating a sudden report.
  • 此猪龙，无能为 - Means 'this pig-dragon is harmless', expressing a dismissive attitude.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 幽陵 - Refers to Youzhou, a region in northern China, which was a significant area during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 禄山 - An Lushan, a prominent military leader who led a rebellion against the Tang Dynasty.
  • 国忠 - Yang Guozhong, a powerful official during the Tang Dynasty, who was involved in the political turmoil leading to the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 虢国 - Yang Guifei's sister, who was also a member of the imperial court.
  • 贵妃 - Yang Guifei, the favorite concubine of Emperor Xuanzong, who played a significant role in the political events leading to the rebellion.
  • 皇太子 - The crown prince, who was the heir to the throne.
  • 东宫 - The palace of the crown prince, symbolizing the future ruler.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong, who later became the emperor after the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 胡旋舞 - A traditional dance performed by people from the Western Regions, known for its fast and intricate movements.
  • 勤政楼 - A famous building in Chang'an (modern-day Xi'an), where the emperor held court and hosted important events.
  • 百戏 - A variety of performances, including acrobatics, music, and dance, often presented at court.

Proper Nouns

  • 禄山 - An Lushan, a prominent military leader.
  • 国忠 - Yang Guozhong, a powerful official.
  • 虢国 - Yang Guifei's sister.
  • 贵妃 - Yang Guifei, the favorite concubine of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 皇太子 - The crown prince, who was the heir to the throne.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong, who later became the emperor.
  • 勤政楼 - A famous building in Chang'an, where the emperor held court.
  • 胡旋舞 - A traditional dance performed by people from the Western Regions.
  • 东宫 - The palace of the crown prince.

[424]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幸巴蜀 - Formal expression meaning 'travel to Bashu' (a region in Sichuan), often used in imperial contexts to indicate a journey for safety or retreat.
  • 右龙武将军 - Title for a high-ranking military official, specifically the commander of the Right Dragon Army, a prestigious military unit.
  • 万乘震荡 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the empire is in turmoil,' where '万乘' (wàn shèng) refers to the emperor, and '震荡' (zhèngdàng) means 'to shake or tremble.'
  • 遮国忠诉事 - Phrase meaning 'to block or intercept Guo Zhong to lodge a complaint,' where '遮' (zhē) means 'to block' and '诉事' (sù shì) means 'to lodge a complaint.'
  • 会吐蕃和好使在驿门 - Phrase meaning 'coincidentally, the Tibetan envoy for peace was at the驿站门 (yì zhàn mén),' where '会' (huì) means 'coincidentally' and '和好使' (hé hǎo shǐ) means 'envoy for peace.'
  • 伏乞圣虑裁断 - Formal expression meaning 'I humbly request the emperor's wise decision,' where '伏乞' (fú qǐ) means 'I humbly request' and '裁断' (cái duàn) means 'to decide or judge.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 潼关失守 - Refers to the fall of Tongguan, a strategic pass in Shaanxi, which was a significant event during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 马嵬 - Refers to Ma嵬, a place in Shaanxi where the famous incident of the death of Yang Guifei occurred.
  • 杨国忠 - A prominent political figure during the Tang Dynasty, known for his controversial policies and influence.
  • 吐蕃 - Refers to the Tibetan Empire, which had diplomatic relations with the Tang Dynasty.
  • 张易之 - A eunuch and political figure during the reign of Empress Wu Zetian, known for his influence and controversial actions.
  • 国忠旧名钊 - Indicates that Yang Guifei's brother, Yang Guozhao, originally had a different name, reflecting the complexity of historical records and identities.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure is elliptical and must be read as a single unit, indicating that the emperor traveled to Bashu, and the princess followed.
  • The sentence structure is complex, with a main clause followed by a subordinate clause, requiring careful parsing.
  • The sentence structure is compact, with a main clause followed by a list of actions, requiring clear separation in English.
  • The sentence structure is elliptical, requiring the addition of implied subjects and actions for clarity.
  • The sentence structure is simple but must be read as a direct quote, requiring careful handling of the dialogue.

Proper Nouns

  • 潼关 - Tongguan, a strategic pass in Shaanxi.
  • 巴蜀 - Bashu, a region in Sichuan.
  • 马嵬 - Ma嵬, a place in Shaanxi.
  • 右龙武将军 - Right Dragon Army General, a military title.
  • 杨国忠 - Yang Guozhong, a prominent political figure.
  • 吐蕃 - Tibetan Empire.
  • 张易之 - Zhang Yizhi, a eunuch and political figure.
  • 国忠旧名钊 - Yang Guozhao, originally named Zhao.
  • 高力士 - Gao Lishi, a eunuch and trusted advisor to the Tang emperors.

[425]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 聖情昏默 - Describes the emperor's emotional state as dim and silent, suggesting a lack of decisive action.
  • 願大家好注 - An expression of hope that the emperor will be well, possibly implying that he should focus on his duties.
  • 妾誠負國恩，死無恨矣 - The empress acknowledges her guilt and expresses no regret at dying.
  • 願妃子善地受生 - A wish that the empress will be reborn in a good place.
  • 此去劍門，鳥啼花落，水綠山青，無非助朕悲悼妃子之由也 - The emperor laments that everything on the journey to Jianmen Pass will remind him of his grief for the empress.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 貴妃 - Refers to Yang Guifei, a famous concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for her beauty and influence.
  • 六軍 - Refers to the imperial guards, which were divided into six units.
  • 繡衾 - A brocade quilt, a luxurious item often associated with high status.
  • 敕 - An imperial edict or order.
  • 玄禮 - General Xuan Li, a military officer who was part of the imperial guard.
  • 劍門 - Jianmen Pass, a strategic location in Sichuan province.
  • 荔枝 - Lychees, a fruit that was highly prized and often sent as a tribute to the emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 貴妃 - Yang Guifei.
  • 六軍 - Imperial guards.
  • 繡衾 - Brocade quilt.
  • 敕 - Imperial edict.
  • 玄禮 - General Xuan Li.
  • 劍門 - Jianmen Pass.
  • 荔枝 - Lychees.
  • 張野狐 - Zhang Yehu (likely a nickname for Zhang Shuo).

[426]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初 - Indicates the beginning or start of a period or event.
  • 上在華清宮日 - Means 'when the emperor was at Huaqing Palace,' introducing the setting.
  • 乘馬出宮門 - Literally 'riding out the palace gate,' indicating an act of leaving the palace.
  • 欲幸虢國夫人之宅 - Means 'wishing to visit the residence of Lady Guo,' where '幸' implies a visit, often with connotations of favor or grace.
  • 未宣敕報臣 - Means 'without announcing the imperial decree to the officials,' highlighting the lack of formal communication.
  • 天子不可輕去就 - A formal statement meaning 'the emperor cannot casually come and go,' emphasizing the importance of protocol.
  • 逼上元 - Means 'near the Lantern Festival,' indicating a specific time of year.
  • 數日 - Means 'several days,' indicating a short period.
  • 雖林甫養育之，國忠激怒之，然其有所自也 - A complex clause meaning 'although Li Linfu raised him and Yang Guozhong provoked him, yet there was a reason behind it,' suggesting a deeper cause beyond immediate influences.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 華清宮 - Refers to Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial retreat in Shaanxi province, known for its hot springs and historical significance.
  • 虢國夫人 - Refers to Lady Guo, a member of the Yang family and a favorite of Emperor Xuanzong, known for her beauty and influence.
  • 李遐周 - A historical figure, a Daoist priest and poet, known for his prophetic verses.
  • 燕市人皆去 - Refers to the historical event where An Lushan, a military general, led a rebellion from the region of Yan (modern-day Beijing).
  • 函關馬不歸 - Refers to the Battle of Tongguan, where the Tang general Geshu Han was defeated by the rebels.
  • 若逢山下鬼 - Refers to the place name 'Mawei,' which is a key location in the historical event known as the Mawei Incident, where Emperor Xuanzong ordered the execution of his favorite concubine, Yang Guifei.
  • 環上系羅衣 - Refers to Yang Guifei, whose nickname was 'Yuhuan,' and the manner of her death, where she was strangled with a sash.
  • 義髻抛河里，黄裙逐水流 - A folk song or proverb that became a prophecy about Yang Guifei's death, where '义髻' (a type of hairpin) and '黄裙' (yellow skirt) symbolize her identity and fate.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 玄礼 - Refers to General Guo Ziyi, who was the commander of the imperial guards.
  • 虢國夫人 - Refers to Lady Guo, a member of the Yang family.
  • 李遐周 - A historical figure, a Daoist priest and poet.
  • 哥舒翰 - A Tang general who was defeated at the Battle of Tongguan.
  • 马嵬驿 - The place where the Mawei Incident occurred.

[427]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 是时 - Refers to 'at that time' or 'then', setting the temporal context.
  • 先至 - Means 'arrived first', indicating arrival before others.
  • 诛问至 - Means 'the news of execution arrived', referring to the arrival of information about the execution.
  • 率吏人 - Means 'led officials and people', indicating leadership and mobilization.
  • 走入 - Means 'ran into', describing movement in a hurry.
  • 以为 - Means 'thought' or 'believed', indicating perception or assumption.
  • 娘子 - A respectful term for a woman, often used for a wife or daughter.
  • 不惜 - Means 'not to spare' or 'not to hesitate', indicating reluctance or hesitation.
  • 方便 - Here means 'a way out' or 'an opportunity', suggesting a chance to escape.
  • 刺杀 - Means 'stabbed and killed', describing a violent act.
  • 自刎 - Means 'committed suicide by cutting one's throat', describing a method of suicide.
  • 血凝其喉 - Means 'blood congealed in her throat', indicating the cause of death.
  • 遂并坎 - Means 'and were buried together', indicating a joint burial.
  • 十余步 - Means 'more than ten steps', indicating distance.
  • 道北 - Means 'on the north side of the road', indicating location.
  • 上发马嵬 - Means 'the emperor departed from Ma嵬', indicating a departure.
  • 行至扶风道 - Means 'traveled to the road of Fufeng', indicating a journey.
  • 端正树 - Means 'upright tree', indicating a tree with a straight trunk.
  • 属霖雨 - Means 'encountered continuous rain', indicating weather conditions.
  • 涉旬 - Means 'lasted for several days', indicating duration.
  • 隔山相应 - Means 'echoing across the mountains', indicating sound transmission.
  • 悼念 - Means 'mourned', indicating grief and remembrance.
  • 采其声 - Means 'collected its sound', indicating the gathering of a sound.
  • 寄恨焉 - Means 'to express sorrow', indicating the purpose of the collection.
  • 收复 - Means 'reclaimed', indicating the recapture of territory.
  • 十一月 - Refers to 'November', indicating a specific month.
  • 自成都还 - Means 'returned from Chengdu', indicating a return journey.
  • 使祭之 - Means 'ordered a sacrifice', indicating a religious ceremony.
  • 改葬 - Means 'reburial', indicating a change in burial location.
  • 李辅国等不从 - Means 'Li Fuguo and others did not agree', indicating opposition.
  • 礼部恃郎 - Refers to 'the Minister of Rites', indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 龙武将士 - Refers to 'the Dragon Army soldiers', indicating a specific military unit.
  • 反 - Means 'rebelled', indicating rebellion.
  • 诛之 - Means 'executed them', indicating punishment.
  • 恐 - Means 'fear', indicating apprehension.
  • 肃宗遂止之 - Means 'Emperor Suzong stopped it', indicating a decision.
  • 密令 - Means 'secretly ordered', indicating a covert action.
  • 中官 - Refers to 'eunuchs', indicating a specific group of officials.
  • 潜移葬之 - Means 'secretly moved the burial', indicating a covert action.
  • 他所 - Means 'another place', indicating a different location.
  • 初瘗 - Means 'initial burial', indicating the first burial.
  • 紫褥裹之 - Means 'wrapped in purple silk', indicating a specific wrapping material.
  • 肌肤已消释 - Means 'flesh had decomposed', indicating decomposition.
  • 胸前犹有锦香囊在焉 - Means 'there was still a brocade perfume bag on her chest', indicating a specific item found on the body.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虢国夫人 - Refers to 'Lady Gou of the State of Guo', a prominent figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陈仓 - Refers to 'Chencang', a county in Shaanxi Province.
  • 县令薛景仙 - Refers to 'County Magistrate Xue Jingxian', a local official.
  • 国忠 - Refers to 'Guo Zhong', a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 裴柔 - Refers to 'Pei Rou', the wife of Guo Zhong.
  • 肃宗 - Refers to 'Emperor Suzong', the emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 上皇 - Refers to 'the Emperor Emeritus', indicating the retired emperor.
  • 李辅国 - Refers to 'Li Fuguo', a powerful eunuch during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 雨霖铃 - Refers to 'Rain Bell', a musical piece composed by the emperor to express his sorrow.

Proper Nouns

  • 虢国夫人 - Lady Gou of the State of Guo.
  • 陈仓 - Chencang.
  • 县令薛景仙 - County Magistrate Xue Jingxian.
  • 国忠 - Guo Zhong.
  • 裴柔 - Pei Rou.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong.
  • 上皇 - Emperor Emeritus.
  • 李辅国 - Li Fuguo.
  • 雨霖铃 - Rain Bell.
  • 至德二年 - 757 AD.
  • 西京 - Western Capital (Chang'an).
  • 成都 - Chengdu.
  • 礼部恃郎 - Minister of Rites.
  • 李揆 - Li Kui.
  • 龙武将士 - Dragon Army soldiers.
  • 杨国忠 - Yang Guozhong.
  • 故妃 - Former Consort.
  • 中官 - Eunuchs.

[428]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 中官葬畢以獻 - After the eunuchs had finished the burial, they presented [something]. This phrase indicates a ritual or formal act of presentation.
  • 上皇置之懷袖 - The emperor placed it in his sleeves. This is a metaphor for keeping something close, symbolizing emotional attachment.
  • 令畫工寫妃形於別殿 - Ordered the painter to depict the queen's likeness in another hall. This suggests a deliberate act of preserving memory.
  • 夜闌登勤政樓 - At the end of the night, he climbed the Hall of Diligent Governance. This sets a specific time and place for the emperor's actions.
  • 憑欄南望，煙月滿目 - Leaning on the railing and looking south, he saw the mist and moonlight everywhere. This evokes a poetic and melancholic atmosphere.
  • 庭前琪樹已堪攀，塞外征人殊未還 - The jade trees in the courtyard are already climbable, but the soldiers on the frontier have not returned. This is a metaphorical expression of time passing and the absence of loved ones.
  • 聞里中隱隱如有歌聲者 - He heard faint singing in the neighborhood. This suggests a mysterious or ethereal presence.
  • 得非梨園舊人乎 - Could it not be an old member of the Pear Garden? This is a rhetorical question, indicating a possibility.
  • 遲明，為我訪來 - In the morning, bring them to me. This indicates a request for the next day.
  • 至德中，復幸華清宮 - In the middle of the Tianbao era, he again visited the Palace of Warm Springs. This marks a specific historical period and location.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 中官 - Refers to eunuchs, who were often involved in imperial rituals and ceremonies.
  • 上皇 - Refers to the emperor, specifically Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 妃 - Refers to the empress or concubine, likely Yang Guifei.
  • 勤政樓 - A hall in the imperial palace where the emperor would conduct official business.
  • 梨園 - A term for the imperial music and dance troupe, known for its performers.
  • 涼州 - A region in the northwest, famous for its music and poetry.
  • 華清宮 - A famous imperial palace and resort, known for its hot springs and luxurious lifestyle.
  • 至德中 - Refers to the middle of the Tianbao era (742-756), a period of prosperity and decline in the Tang dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 中官 - Eunuchs.
  • 上皇 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 妃 - Yang Guifei.
  • 勤政樓 - Hall of Diligent Governance.
  • 梨園 - Imperial music and dance troupe.
  • 涼州 - Region in the northwest.
  • 華清宮 - Palace of Warm Springs.
  • 至德中 - Middle of the Tianbao era.

[429]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 从官嫔御 - Refers to the attendants and concubines who serve the emperor.
  • 多非旧人 - Means 'mostly not old acquaintances,' indicating that many of these attendants are newcomers.
  • 上于望京楼下命张野狐奏《雨霖铃》曲 - '上' refers to the emperor, and '命' means 'commanded.' '张野狐' is the name of a musician, and '《雨霖铃》' is the name of a musical piece.
  • 曲半，上四顾凄凉，不觉流涕 - '四顾' means 'looked around,' and '凄凉' conveys a sense of desolation or sadness.
  • 左右亦为感伤 - '左右' refers to those around the emperor, and '感伤' means 'moved to sadness.'
  • 善舞《凌波曲》 - '善' means 'skilled at,' and '《凌波曲》' is the name of a dance.
  • 旧出入宫禁 - Means 'frequently entered and left the palace,' indicating close association with the imperial court.
  • 贵妃厚焉 - '厚焉' means 'treated her well,' referring to the favor shown by the empress.
  • 朕以岐王所进《龙池篇》，赐之金带，红玉支赐妃子 - '朕' is the first-person pronoun for the emperor, '岐王' is the name of a prince, and '《龙池篇》' is a literary work. '金带' and '红玉支' are valuable gifts.
  • 朕寻以为得此不足为贵，乃命还其紫金带 - '寻' means 'soon,' '以为' means 'thought,' and '不足为贵' means 'not worth much.' '乃命还其紫金带' means 'then ordered the return of the purple gold belt.'
  • 惟此不还 - '惟此' means 'only this,' and '不还' means 'not returned.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 望京楼 - A palace building where the emperor might have gathered his court.
  • 张野狐 - A historical musician known for his skill.
  • 《雨霖铃》 - A musical piece associated with rain and lamentation.
  • 谢阿蛮 - A historical female performer known for her dancing.
  • 《凌波曲》 - A dance that was popular at the time.
  • 贵妃 - Refers to a high-ranking concubine, likely Yang Guifei, a famous concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 岐王 - A prince, possibly Li Longji (Emperor Xuanzong) before he became emperor.
  • 《龙池篇》 - A literary work that was presented to the emperor.
  • 高丽 - An ancient kingdom in Korea.
  • 紫金带 - A purple gold belt, a symbol of high status and power.

Proper Nouns

  • 望京楼 - A palace building.
  • 张野狐 - A historical musician.
  • 《雨霖铃》 - A musical piece.
  • 谢阿蛮 - A historical female performer.
  • 《凌波曲》 - A dance.
  • 贵妃 - A high-ranking concubine.
  • 岐王 - A prince.
  • 《龙池篇》 - A literary work.
  • 高丽 - An ancient kingdom in Korea.
  • 紫金带 - A purple gold belt.

[430]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 汝既得之于妃子 - 汝 means 'you,' 既得之 means 'having received it,' 于妃子 means 'from the concubine.' This phrase indicates that the speaker has received something from the concubine.
  • 朕今再睹之 - 朕 means 'I' (emperor), 今 means 'now,' 再睹之 means 'to see it again.' This phrase indicates that the emperor is seeing something again.
  • 但兴悲念矣 - 但 means 'only' or 'just,' 兴悲念 means 'to arouse sorrowful thoughts,' 矣 is a particle indicating completion or certainty. This phrase indicates that the emperor is only feeling sorrowful thoughts.
  • 言讫，又涕零 - 言讫 means 'having finished speaking,' 又涕零 means 'again shedding tears.' This phrase indicates that after finishing his speech, the emperor again sheds tears.
  • 至乾元元年 - 至 means 'until' or 'up to,' 乾元元年 is the first year of the Qianyuan era, a period in Chinese history. This phrase sets the time frame for the events described.
  • 贺怀智又上言 - 贺怀智 is the name of a person, 又上言 means 'again presented a statement.' This phrase indicates that He Huizhi presented another statement.
  • 昔上夏日与亲王棋 - 昔 means 'in the past,' 上 refers to the emperor, 夏日 means 'in the summer,' 与 means 'with,' 亲王 means 'prince,' 棋 means 'to play chess.' This phrase describes a past event where the emperor played chess with a prince.
  • 令臣独弹琵琶 - 令 means 'to order,' 臣 means 'I' (referring to the speaker), 独 means 'alone,' 弹琵琶 means 'to play the pipa.' This phrase indicates that the emperor ordered the speaker to play the pipa alone.
  • 其琵琶以石为槽，鸡筋为弦，用铁拨弹之 - 其 means 'its,' 琵琶 means 'pipa,' 以石为槽 means 'with a stone body,' 鸡筋为弦 means 'with chicken tendons as strings,' 用铁拨弹之 means 'played with an iron plectrum.' This phrase describes the unique construction of the pipa.
  • 贵妃立于局前观之 - 贵妃 means 'the concubine,' 立于局前 means 'standing in front of the board,' 观之 means 'watching it.' This phrase describes the concubine standing in front of the chessboard watching the game.
  • 上数枰子将输 - 上 refers to the emperor, 数枰子 means 'counting the pieces,' 将输 means 'about to lose.' This phrase indicates that the emperor was about to lose the game.
  • 贵妃放康国子上局乱之 - 贵妃 means 'the concubine,' 放 means 'to let,' 康国子 means 'a boy from Kangguo,' 上局 means 'on the board,' 乱之 means 'to disrupt it.' This phrase indicates that the concubine let a boy from Kangguo disrupt the board.
  • 上大悦 - 上 refers to the emperor, 大悦 means 'greatly pleased.' This phrase indicates that the emperor was greatly pleased.
  • 时风吹贵妃领巾于臣巾上 - 时 means 'at that time,' 风 means 'wind,' 吹 means 'blowing,' 贵妃领巾 means 'the concubine's scarf,' 于 means 'onto,' 臣巾上 means 'on my headscarf.' This phrase describes the wind blowing the concubine's scarf onto the speaker's headscarf.
  • 良久，回身方落 - 良久 means 'for a long time,' 回身 means 'turning back,' 方落 means 'finally fell.' This phrase indicates that after a long time, the scarf finally fell.
  • 及归，觉满身香气 - 及 means 'when,' 归 means 'returning,' 觉 means 'felt,' 满身香气 means 'the whole body filled with fragrance.' This phrase describes the speaker feeling the whole body filled with fragrance when returning.
  • 乃卸头帻，贮于锦囊中 - 乃 means 'then,' 卸 means 'removed,' 头帻 means 'headscarf,' 贮 means 'stored,' 于 means 'in,' 锦囊中 means 'in a brocade bag.' This phrase indicates that the speaker then removed the headscarf and stored it in a brocade bag.
  • 今辄进所贮幞头 - 今 means 'now,' 辄 means 'immediately,' 进 means 'to present,' 所贮 means 'what is stored,' 枹头 means 'headscarf.' This phrase indicates that the speaker immediately presented the stored headscarf.
  • 上皇发囊，且曰 - 上皇 means 'the former emperor,' 发囊 means 'opened the bag,' 且曰 means 'and said.' This phrase indicates that the former emperor opened the bag and said something.
  • 此瑞龙脑香也 - 此 means 'this,' 瑞龙脑香 means 'precious frankincense.' This phrase identifies the fragrance as precious frankincense.
  • 吾曾施于暖池玉莲朵 - 吾 means 'I,' 曾 means 'once,' 施 means 'applied,' 于 means 'to,' 暖池 means 'warm pool,' 玉莲朵 means 'lotus flower.' This phrase indicates that the former emperor once applied the fragrance to a lotus flower in a warm pool.
  • 再幸尚有香气宛然 - 再幸 means 'again when visited,' 尚有 means 'still has,' 香气 means 'fragrance,' 宛然 means 'just as before.' This phrase indicates that the fragrance still remained just as before.
  • 况乎丝缕润腻之物哉 - 况乎 means 'how much more,' 丝缕 means 'threads,' 润腻 means 'smooth and lustrous,' 之物 means 'such things.' This phrase indicates how much more so for smooth and lustrous things.
  • 遂凄伦不已 - 遂 means 'then,' 凄伦 means 'grieved and mournful,' 不已 means 'without end.' This phrase indicates that the former emperor then grieved and mournful without end.
  • 自是圣怀耿耿 - 自是 means 'from then on,' 圣怀 means 'the heart of the sage,' 耿耿 means 'restless.' This phrase indicates that from then on, the heart of the sage was restless.
  • 但吟 - 但 means 'only,' 吟 means 'to recite.' This phrase indicates that the former emperor only recited poetry.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及至移入大内甘露殿 - Means 'when he was moved into the Ganlu Hall in the Inner Palace.' Sets the scene for the emperor's actions and emotions.
  • 悲悼妃子 - Means 'to grieve deeply for the concubine.' Conveys the emperor's profound sorrow.
  • 辟谷服气 - Refers to the practice of abstaining from food and breathing exercises, often associated with Daoist practices.
  • 张皇后进樱桃蔗浆 - Means 'Empress Zhang offered him cherry and sugarcane juice.' Indicates the empress's attempt to comfort the emperor.
  • 圣皇并不食 - Means 'the Holy Emperor did not eat.' Emphasizes the emperor's refusal to eat despite the empress's efforts.
  • 因吹数声 - Means 'he blew a few notes.' Describes the emperor's action with the flute.
  • 有双鹤下于庭，徘徊而去 - Means 'two cranes descended into the courtyard and then left.' Symbolic event indicating the emperor's spiritual transformation.
  • 吾奉上帝所命，为元始孔升真人 - Means 'I am commanded by the Heavenly Deity to become the True Person Kongsheng of the Primordial Origin.' Statement of the emperor's new spiritual identity.
  • 此期可再会妃子耳 - Means 'this time I can only meet the concubine again.' Expresses the emperor's regret and the finality of their separation.
  • 笛非尔所宝，可送大收 - Means 'the flute is not something you should treasure; give it to Dashi.' Directive to the servant.
  • 即令具汤沐 - Means 'he immediately ordered to prepare a bath.' Indicates the emperor's preparation for his spiritual transformation.
  • 我若就枕，慎勿惊我 - Means 'if I lie down, do not disturb me.' Final instruction to the servant.
  • 宫爱闻睡中有声，骇而视之，已崩矣 - Means 'Palace Love heard a sound in his sleep, was startled and looked, and found that he had already passed away.' Describes the servant's reaction to the emperor's death.
  • 妃子死日，马嵬媪得锦拗袜一只 - Means 'on the day the concubine died, an old woman at Mawei obtained one silk-bound sock.' Reference to the aftermath of the concubine's death.
  • 相传过客一玩百钱，前后获钱无数 - Means 'it is said that each visitor would pay a hundred coins to see it, and the old woman earned countless coins.' Describes the old woman's financial gain from the sock.
  • 悲夫 - Expression of sorrow or lamentation.
  • 玄宗在位久，倦于万机 - Means 'Emperor Xuanzong had been on the throne for a long time and was tired of the myriad affairs of state.'
  • 常以大臣接对拘检 - Means 'he was often constrained by his interactions with ministers.'
  • 难徇私欲 - Means 'it was difficult to indulge in personal desires.'
  • 自得李林甫，一以委成 - Means 'since he obtained Li Linfu, he entrusted everything to him.'
  • 故绝逆耳之言，恣行燕乐 - Means 'thus he cut off advice that was unpleasant to hear and indulged in feasts and music.'
  • 衽席无别，不以为耻 - Means 'there was no distinction in his private life, and he did not consider it shameful.'
  • 申林甫之赞成矣 - Means 'this was in line with Li Linfu's support.'
  • 乘舆迁播，朝廷陷没 - Means 'the imperial carriage was forced to flee, and the court was captured.'
  • 百僚系颈，妃王被戮 - Means 'all officials were executed, and the concubines and princes were killed.'
  • 兵满天下，毒流四海 - Describes the widespread chaos and suffering caused by the rebellion.
  • 皆国忠之召祸也 - Means 'all of this was the result of Guo Zong's misfortune.'

  • 俄有二龙，帝跨白龙，其去若飞，妃跨黑龙，其行甚缓 - Appearance of two dragons, with the emperor riding a white dragon that flies away quickly and the empress riding a black dragon that moves slowly, highlighting contrast in speed and color.
  • 左右无人，惟一蓬头面物，貌不类人，望帝去之甚远，触一危峰，沉烟蔼中 - Scene where there are no attendants, only a creature with disheveled hair and a face unlike a human, watching the emperor fly far away and eventually touching a dangerous peak and disappearing into a cloud of smoke, adding a mystical element to the dream.
  • 开目，则独自一室，面物曰：“某此峰神也。” - Awakening from the dream, where the empress finds herself alone in a room, and the creature identifies itself as the spirit of the mountain peak.

  • 悠然梦觉，翌日，渔阳叛书至 - Peaceful awakening from the dream and the next day's news of a rebellion in Yuyang, contrasting the dream and reality.
  • 帝至马嵬缢妃子死 - Emperor's journey to Mawei and the subsequent death of the empress by hanging, historically significant and emotionally charged.
  • 梦今有应矣。与朕游骊山。骊与离同；方食火发，失食之兆。火，兵器也。驿木俱焚，驿与易同，加木于旁，杨字也。吾跨白龙，西游之象。彼跨黑龙，阴暗之理。独行无左右之助，一骑马也。峰神，乃山鬼也，果死于马嵬乎。当授益州牧蚕元后，牧，养也；养蚕所以致丝也，益旁加丝，缢字也。 - Detailed interpretation of the dream using symbolic language to predict the empress's fate, involving various classical references and symbolic meanings.

  • 驚鴻舞 - Dance move inspired by the startled swan, symbolizing elegance and grace.
  • 一座光輝 - Means 'the entire gathering was illuminated'; indicates the concubine's ability to captivate everyone.
  • 斗茶 - Ritualized tea competition, where participants compare the quality of their tea.
  • 草木之戲 - Humble way of referring to the tea competition, meaning 'a game of plants and trees'.
  • 烹任鼎鼐 - Refers to cooking in large cauldrons, symbolizing governance and administration.
  • 萬乘 - Term for the emperor, derived from the idea of a chariot drawn by a thousand horses, symbolizing imperial power.
  • 賤妾 - Humble self-reference by a concubine, meaning 'lowly wife'.
  • 憲法 - Refers to the established rules or laws of the state.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 承平歲久 reflects the long period of peace and stability during the reign of Emperor Huizong of Song (1100-1125), a time of cultural and artistic flourishing.
  • 海内無事 indicates the absence of major internal conflicts or external threats during this period.
  • 友愛 highlights the close relationship between the emperor and his brothers, which was often emphasized in imperial court culture.
  • 妃侍側 describes the luxurious and indulgent lifestyle of the imperial court, where concubines and attendants were always present.
  • 破橙往賜諸王 is a ritual act that symbolized the emperor's favor and generosity towards his princes.
  • 梅精 is a term often used to describe a beautiful and talented woman, reflecting the cultural appreciation for feminine grace and elegance.
  • 驚鴻舞 is a dance move that symbolized the beauty and grace of a woman, often used in literature to describe a woman's movements.
  • 斗茶 is a popular pastime among the elite, where participants would compare the quality of their tea, often using elaborate rituals and utensils.
  • 四海調和 is a metaphor for effective governance, where the emperor is seen as the one who harmonizes the entire empire.
  • 烹任鼎鼐 is a metaphor for the emperor's role in governing the state, where he is seen as the one who manages the affairs of the nation.

  • 斗茶 - Ritualized tea competition, a popular pastime among the elite.

  • 賤妾 - Humble self-reference by a concubine, meaning 'lowly wife'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玉鉴尘生 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'dust accumulates on the jade mirror,' symbolizing neglect or decay.
  • 凤奁香珍 - Refers to a precious casket filled with fragrant items, often used to describe a woman's beauty or luxury.
  • 懒蝉鬓之巧梳 - Describes a woman who is too lazy to comb her delicate hair, symbolizing neglect of personal appearance.
  • 闲缕衣之轻练 - Refers to a woman who idly threads silk, symbolizing idleness or lack of purpose.
  • 苦寂寞于蕙宫 - Means 'suffering loneliness in the fragrant palace,' where 蕙宫 is a metaphor for a secluded or lonely place.
  • 但凝思乎兰殿 - Means 'only thinking deeply in the fragrant hall,' where 兰殿 is another metaphor for a place of seclusion or contemplation.
  • 信标落之梅花 - Means 'trusting the falling plum blossoms as a sign,' symbolizing the passage of time or the inevitability of decline.
  • 隔长门而不见 - Refers to being separated by the long gate, symbolizing isolation or separation from a loved one.
  • 况乃花心恨 - Means 'how much more the sorrow of the flower heart,' where 花心 symbolizes a woman's heart or emotions.
  • 柳眼弄愁 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the willow's eyes play with sorrow,' symbolizing the willow's branches as a source of melancholy.
  • 暖风习习 - Describes a gentle, warm breeze, often used to evoke a sense of comfort or nostalgia.
  • 春鸟啾啾 - Refers to the chirping of spring birds, symbolizing the arrival of spring or a sense of renewal.
  • 楼上黄昏兮，听风吹而回首 - Describes a woman on the upper floor of a building at dusk, listening to the wind and turning back, symbolizing longing or reflection.
  • 碧云日暮兮，对素月而凝眸 - Describes the evening sky with blue clouds and the pale moon, symbolizing solitude and contemplation.
  • 温泉不到，忆拾翠之旧游 - Means 'the hot springs are inaccessible, recalling past excursions,' where 拾翠 refers to past pleasures or adventures.
  • 长门深闭，嗟青鸾之信修 - Means 'the long gate is deeply closed, lamenting the long journey of the blue phoenix,' where 青鸾 symbolizes a messenger or a loved one.
  • 忆太液清波，水光荡浮 - Refers to memories of the clear waters of Taiye Lake, symbolizing past beauty or tranquility.
  • 笙歌赏宴，陪从宸旒 - Describes musical performances and feasts attended by the emperor, symbolizing royal splendor or luxury.
  • 奏舞鸾之妙曲，乘画之仙舟 - Describes playing the exquisite music of the dancing phoenix and riding a painted boat, symbolizing elegance or fantasy.
  • 君情缱绻，深叙绸缪 - Means 'the lord's affection is deep and intricate,' where 缱绻 and 绸缪 describe intense, intertwined emotions.
  • 誓山海而常在，似日月而亡休 - Means 'swearing to remain as constant as the mountains and seas, like the sun and moon without rest,' symbolizing eternal love or loyalty.
  • 奈何嫉色庸庸，妒气冲冲 - Means 'how can one bear the jealous and envious nature,' where 嫉色庸庸 and 妒气冲冲 describe jealousy and envy.
  • 夺我之爱幸，斥我乎幽宫 - Means 'taking away my beloved, banishing me to a secluded palace,' symbolizing betrayal or abandonment.
  • 思旧欢之莫得，想梦著乎朦陇 - Means 'thinking of past joys that cannot be regained, dreaming in a hazy state,' where 朦陇 describes a dreamlike or confused state.
  • 度花朝与月夕，羞懒对乎春风 - Means 'passing the flower festival and moonlit nights, feeling ashamed and lazy in the spring breeze,' symbolizing the passage of time and changing emotions.
  • 欲相如之奏赋，奈世才之不工 - Means 'wanting to compose like Xiangru, but lamenting the world's lack of talent,' where Xiangru refers to Sima Xiangru, a famous poet.
  • 属愁吟之未尽，已响动乎疏钟 - Means 'while the sorrowful singing is not yet finished, the distant bell has already sounded,' symbolizing the passage of time or the inevitability of change.
  • 空长叹而掩袂，踌躇步于楼东 - Means 'in vain sighing and covering one's sleeves, hesitatingly walking to the east of the building,' symbolizing sadness or indecision.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 玉鉴 - A jade mirror, often used as a metaphor for purity or reflection.
  • 凤奁 - A casket or box, often associated with a woman's beauty or luxury.
  • 蝉鬓 - A delicate hairstyle, often used to describe a woman's beauty.
  • 蕙宫 - A fragrant palace, often used as a metaphor for a secluded or lonely place.
  • 兰殿 - A fragrant hall, often used as a metaphor for a place of seclusion or contemplation.
  • 梅花 - Plum blossoms, often used to symbolize resilience, beauty, or the passage of time.
  • 长门 - A long gate, often used to symbolize separation or isolation.
  • 太液 - Taiye Lake, a famous lake in ancient China, often used to symbolize tranquility or beauty.
  • 宸旒 - The emperor, often used to symbolize royal authority or splendor.
  • 舞鸾 - A dancing phoenix, often used to symbolize elegance or fantasy.
  • 相如 - Sima Xiangru, a famous poet and musician from the Han dynasty, often used as a symbol of literary talent.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This opening couplet sets the tone for the poem, describing neglect (玉鉴尘生) and luxury (凤奁香珍).
  • This couplet describes a woman's neglect of her appearance, using the metaphors of a delicate hairstyle (蝉鬓) and silk (缕衣).
  • This couplet describes the woman's loneliness and contemplation, using the metaphors of a fragrant palace (蕙宫) and a fragrant hall (兰殿).
  • This couplet describes the passage of time and separation, using the metaphors of falling plum blossoms (梅花) and a long gate (长门).
  • This couplet describes the woman's sorrow, using the metaphors of a flower heart (花心) and willow eyes (柳眼).
  • This couplet describes the arrival of spring, using the metaphors of a gentle breeze (暖风) and spring birds (春鸟).
  • This couplet describes the woman's longing, using the metaphors of a building (楼) and a wind (风).
  • This couplet describes the evening sky, using the metaphors of blue clouds (碧云) and a pale moon (素月).
  • This couplet describes the woman's memories, using the metaphors of hot springs (温泉) and past excursions (拾翠).
  • This couplet describes the woman's isolation, using the metaphors of a long gate (长门) and a blue phoenix (青鸾).
  • This couplet describes the woman's memories of Taiye Lake, using the metaphors of clear waters (太液清波) and water ripples (水光荡浮).
  • This couplet describes royal splendor, using the metaphors of musical performances (笙歌) and feasts (赏宴).
  • This couplet describes elegance or fantasy, using the metaphors of a dancing phoenix (舞鸾) and a painted boat (画舟).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 長門自是無梳洗 - Ambiguous phrase; could mean 'Long Gate has long since ceased to be a place of grooming' or 'Long Gate is no longer a place of grooming'; refers to the decline of the palace after the emperor's favor shifted.
  • 何必珍珠慰寂寞 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why bother with pearls to comfort loneliness?'; implies that material wealth cannot compensate for emotional loss.
  • 上覽詩，怅然不樂 - The emperor, upon reading the poem, felt melancholic and unhappy; '怅然不樂' conveys a deep sense of sorrow and dissatisfaction.
  • 令樂府以新聲度之，號《一斛珠》，曲名是此始 - He ordered the Music Bureau to compose a new tune based on the poem, naming it 'One Measure of Pearls,' and this was the first time the name was used; '以新聲度之' means to set the poem to a new melody.
  • 後禄山犯闭，上西幸，太真死 - After An Lushan rebelled, the emperor fled westward, and Lady Yang died; '犯闭' refers to the rebellion by An Lushan.
  • 及东归，寻妃所在，不可得 - When he returned eastward, he could not find the whereabouts of his concubine; '寻妃所在' means to search for the location of the concubine.
  • 诏：“有得之，官二秩，钱百万。” - He issued an edict: 'Anyone who finds her will be granted two ranks of office and one million coins'; '官二秩' means to be promoted to two ranks higher.
  • 命方士飞神御气，潜经天地，亦不可得 - He ordered the Daoist priests to fly their spirits and control their breaths, searching through heaven and earth, but still could not find her; '飞神御气' means to fly the spirit and control the breath.
  • 有宦者进其画真，上言：“甚似，但不活耳。” - A eunuch presented a portrait of her, and the emperor said, 'It looks very much like her, but she is not alive'; '甚似' means very similar.
  • 诗题于上，曰 - The poem was inscribed on it, saying; '诗题于上' means the poem was written on the portrait.
  • 忆昔娇妃在紫宸，铅华不御得天真 - Remembering when the beautiful concubine was in the Purple Palace, she did not use makeup and thus revealed her natural beauty; '铅华不御' means not using makeup.
  • 霜绡虽似当时态，争奈娇波不顾人 - Though the silken scarf resembles her former state, how can it capture her charming gaze that no longer looks at anyone; '霜绡' refers to a fine, white silk scarf.
  • 上悲，谓兵火之后，流落他处 - The emperor was sad, thinking that after the war, she had been scattered to other places; '兵火之后' means after the war.
  • 上益不乐 - The emperor became even more unhappy; '益不乐' means increasingly unhappy.
  • 忽悟温泉汤池侧有梅十余株，岂在是乎 - Suddenly, he realized that there were more than ten plum trees by the hot spring bathhouse, could she be there; '忽悟' means suddenly realized.
  • 上自命驾，令发现 - The emperor personally ordered the search to begin; '自命驾' means personally ordered.
  • 才数株，得尸，裹以锦，盛以酒槽，附土三尺许 - After searching a few trees, they found her body, wrapped in silk, placed in a wine vat, and covered with about three feet of soil; '裹以锦' means wrapped in silk.
  • 上大恸，左右莫能仰视 - The emperor wept bitterly, and those around him could not bear to look up; '大恸' means wept bitterly.
  • 视其所伤，胁下有刀痕 - Upon examining her injuries, there was a knife wound on her side; '胁下有刀痕' means a knife wound on her side.
  • 上自制文诔之，以妃札易葬焉 - The emperor himself wrote an elegy for her and reburied her with the proper honors; '以妃札易葬焉' means reburied her with the proper honors.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 長門 - Refers to the Long Gate Palace, a palace in the Han dynasty.
  • 上 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, who ruled during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 太真 - Refers to Yang Guifei, a famous concubine of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 禄山 - Refers to An Lushan, a military general who rebelled against the Tang dynasty.

  • 宦者 - Refers to eunuchs, who were often involved in imperial administration.
  • 畫真 - Refers to a portrait or likeness.
  • 溫泉湯池 - Refers to a hot spring bathhouse, a place of leisure and relaxation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明皇自为潞州别驾，以豪伟闻 - Emperor Xuanzong was appointed as a deputy governor of Lü Prefecture and was known for his grandeur and magnificence.
  • 用此起支庶，践尊位 - He rose from a minor branch of the imperial family and ascended to the throne.
  • 五十余年，享天下之奉，穷奢极侈 - He enjoyed the wealth of the empire and lived in extreme luxury for over fifty years.
  • 子孙百数，其阅万方美色众矣 - He had numerous descendants and access to countless beautiful women.
  • 晚得杨氏，变易三纲，浊乱四海 - He later obtained Yang Guifei, altered the traditional social order, and caused chaos throughout the empire.
  • 身废国辱，思之不少悔 - He suffered personal disgrace and national humiliation and did not regret it much.
  • 是固有以中其心，满其欲矣 - This was indeed due to something that satisfied his heart and fulfilled his desires.
  • 江妃者，后先其间，以色为所深嫉 - Jiang Wanli was among the concubines before and after Yang Guifei and was deeply resented for her beauty.
  • 议者谓：或覆宗，或非命，均其媚忌自取 - Commentators say that some brought down their clans, some met untimely deaths, and both were self-inflicted through flattery and jealousy.
  • 殊不知明皇耄而忮忍，至一日杀三子，如轻断蝼蚁之命 - They did not realize that Emperor Xuanzong became old and resentful, killed three of his sons in one day, and treated their lives as if they were insignificant.
  • 奔窜而归，受制昏逆，四顾嫔嫱，斩亡俱尽，穷独苟活，天下哀之 - He fled back, was controlled by his son, looked around at his concubines, saw them all killed, lived in isolation and misery, and the world pitied him.
  • 《传》曰“以其所不爱及其所爱”，盖天所以酬之也 - As it is said in the *Classic of History*, to punish what one dislikes and reward what one loves is how heaven rewards or punishes.
  • 报复之理，毫发不差，是岂特两女子之罪哉 - The principle of retribution without any deviation is not just the fault of two women.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 明皇 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a significant figure in Chinese history known for his reign during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 潞州 - Refers to Lü Prefecture, a region in modern-day Shanxi Province.
  • 别驾 - A deputy governor in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杨氏 - Refers to Yang Guifei, one of the most famous concubines of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 江妃 - Refers to Jiang Wanli, another concubine of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 三纲 - Refers to the traditional social order of father-son, ruler-subject, and husband-wife relationships.
  • 四海 - Refers to the entire empire or the world.
  • 《传》 - Refers to the *Classic of History*, an ancient Chinese historical text.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 东贡舞女二人 - Refers to two dancing girls offered as tribute from the east.
  • 修眉伙首 - Describes the girls' appearance with 'long and beautiful eyebrows' and 'exquisite/refined.'
  • 兰气融冶 - Symbolizes the girls' graceful demeanor with 'fragrance of orchids' and 'harmonious.'
  • 冬不纩衣，夏无汗体 - Indicates the girls' extraordinary physical condition, not wearing wool in winter and not sweating in summer.
  • 所食多荔枝、榧实、金屑，龙脑之类 - Lists the girls' diet, including lychees, pine nuts, gold powder, and musk, which were luxurious and rare.
  • 戴轻金雅冠，罗衣，无缝而成 - Describes the girls' attire with a 'light gold elegant crown,' 'silk robe,' and 'seamless and perfectly crafted.'
  • 其文织巧，人未之识 - Describes the robe's intricate weaving, with patterns no one has been able to identify.
  • 轻金冠以金丝结之，为鸾凤之状 - Describes the crown's design, made with gold threads and shaped like phoenixes.
  • 仍饰以五彩细珠，玲珑相续可高一尺 - Describes the crown's decoration with multicolored small beads, delicately arranged and about one foot tall.
  • 称之为三二分 - Refers to the crown's size or weight, possibly 'three or two parts' or 'a fraction.'
  • 上更琢玉芙蓉以为二女歌舞台 - Describes the emperor's creation of a jade lotus stage for the girls to sing and dance.
  • 每夜歌舞一发，如鸾凤之音 - Describes the girls' performance, with their dancing and singing like the sound of phoenixes.
  • 百鸟莫不翔集其上，及于庭际 - Describes the magical effect of their performance, causing birds to gather on the stage and even reach the courtyard.
  • 舞态艳逸，非人间所有 - Emphasizes the otherworldly nature of their dance, which was gorgeous and unrestrained.
  • 每歌罢，上令内人藏之金屋宝帐 - Describes the emperor's protection of the girls, hiding them in a golden house and precious tent.
  • 盖恐风日故也 - Explains the reason for their protection, fearing the wind and sun.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宝历二年 - Era name '宝历' (Bao Li) refers to the reign of Emperor Jingzong of Tang, who ruled from 824 to 827.
  • 东贡舞女 - Practice of receiving tribute from the eastern regions, common during the Tang Dynasty, reflecting cultural exchange and tribute system.
  • 琢玉芙蓉 - Carving of a jade lotus as a stage for the girls to perform on, reflecting artistic and cultural sophistication of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 金屋宝帐 - Use of a golden house and precious tent to protect the girls, reflecting imperial court's emphasis on luxury and protection of precious individuals.

Proper Nouns

  • 宝历二年 - Era name '宝历' (Bao Li) and year '二年' (second year), indicating specific time period of the event.
  • 荔枝、榧实 - Specific foods, including lychees and pine nuts, which were luxurious and rare.
  • 轻金雅冠 - Light gold elegant crown worn by the girls.
  • 罗衣 - Silk robe worn by the girls.
  • 无缝而成 - Seamless and perfectly crafted robe worn by the girls.
  • 五彩细珠 - Multicolored small beads used to decorate the crown.
  • 玲珑相续 - Delicately arranged decoration on the crown.
  • 三二分 - Possible reference to the crown's size or weight, 'three or two parts' or 'a fraction.'
  • 琢玉芙蓉 - Jade lotus carved as a stage for the girls to perform on.
  • 金屋宝帐 - Golden house and precious tent used to protect the girls.
  • 宝帐香重重 - Precious tent filled with fragrant layers, describing the palace's admiration of the girls.
  • 一双红芙蓉 - Two red lotuses, describing the palace's admiration of the girls.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大和九年 - Specific year in the Tang Dynasty, corresponding to 835 CE.
  • 诛王涯、郑注后 - After the execution of Wang Ya and Zheng Zhu, a significant political event in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 仇士良专权恣意 - Chou Shiliang monopolized power and acted arbitrarily during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 上颇恶之 - The emperor, Emperor Wenzong, greatly disliked Chou Shiliang's behavior.
  • 或登临游幸 - Refers to the emperor's leisure activities, such as visiting scenic spots.
  • 虽百戏骈罗 - Various entertainments and performances were arranged for the emperor's enjoyment.
  • 未尝以为乐 - Despite the entertainment, the emperor never found true enjoyment.
  • 左右莫敢进问 - The courtiers were too afraid to approach and ask about the emperor's state of mind.
  • 因题曰 - The emperor wrote a poem to express his feelings.
  • 替路生春草，上林花满枝 - Poetic descriptions of the natural scenery.
  • 凭高何限意，无复侍臣知 - Expresses the emperor's sense of isolation and the limitations of his position.
  • 偶于内殿前看牡丹 - The emperor was looking at peonies in front of the inner palace.
  • 翘足凭栏 - The emperor stood on tiptoe leaning on the railing, a posture that suggests contemplation.
  • 忽吟舒元舆《牡丹赋》云 - The emperor recited a line from Shu Yuanyu's *Peony Ode*.
  • 吟罢，方省元舆词 - The emperor realized the line was from Shu Yuanyu's work.
  • 不觉叹息 - The emperor sighed unconsciously, expressing his emotions.
  • 时有宫人沈阿翘 - Shen Aqiao was a palace woman who performed for the emperor.
  • 为上舞《河满子》 - Shen Aqiao danced the *He Manzi* for the emperor.
  • 调声风态，卒皆宛畅 - Her performance was graceful and smooth.
  • 曲罢，上赐金臂环 - The emperor rewarded her with a gold arm ring.
  • 即问其从来 - The emperor asked about her background.
  • 阿翘曰 - Shen Aqiao replied, providing her background.
  • 妾本吴元济之伎女 - She was originally a courtesan of Wu Yuanji.
  • 济败，因以声得为宫人 - She became a palace woman through her singing after Wu Yuanji's defeat.
  • 俄又进白玉方响 - Shen Aqiao presented a white jade flat chime.
  • 光明皎洁，可照十数步 - The chime was bright and pure, shining for dozens of steps.
  • 言犀，捶即响犀也 - The chime was made of rhinoceros horn.
  • 凡物有声，乃响应其中焉 - The chime's properties allowed it to resonate with any sound.
  • 架则云檀香也 - The frame of the chime was made of sandalwood.
  • 而文彩若云霞之状 - The pattern on the chime was like clouds and rosy clouds.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 失其世 - Literally 'lost her family background,' meaning her origins are unknown.
  • 善歌舞 - Means 'skilled in dance and song.'
  • 阴为助画 - Means 'secretly assisted in planning,' suggesting she played a significant role in political matters.
  • 状纤颀 - Describes her physical appearance as slender and tall.
  • 颇类帝 - Means 'resembled the emperor greatly.'
  • 每畋苑中 - 'Whenever hunting in the imperial garden.'
  • 袍而骑 - 'Wearing a robe and riding,' describing her attire and mode of transportation.
  • 容服光宠 - Means 'dressed in radiant and honored garments.'
  • 略同至尊 - 'Almost identical to the emperor's attire,' emphasizing her high status.
  • 相与驰出入 - 'Riding in and out together,' indicating they moved about the garden together.
  • 观者莫知孰为帝也 - 'Observers could not tell who was the emperor,' highlighting the similarity between them.
  • 陛下日燎丹 - 'Your Majesty daily consumes elixirs.'
  • 意取不死 - 'With the intention of achieving immortality.'
  • 肤泽稍槁 - 'Your complexion has slightly faded.'
  • 吾心忧之 - 'I am worried about it.'
  • 陛下万岁后，妾得以殉 - 'After Your Majesty passes away, I will follow you in death.'
  • 陛下大福未艾 - 'Your Majesty's great fortune is not yet exhausted.'
  • 安语不祥 - 'Why speak of ill omens?'
  • 脱如我言 - 'If what I say is true.'
  • 奈何 - 'What should be done?'
  • 审帝已崩 - 'After confirming the emperor had passed away.'
  • 即自经幄下 - 'She immediately hanged herself in the tent.'
  • 当时嫔媛 - 'At that time, the consorts and ladies-in-waiting.'
  • 虽常妒才人专上者 - 'Although they often envied the Cai Ren for her favor.'
  • 返皆义才人 - 'They all admired her.'
  • 为之感恸 - 'They were deeply moved.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 武宗 - Refers to Emperor Wuzong, a ruler of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 穆宗 - Refers to Emperor Muzong, another Tang Dynasty emperor.
  • 颖王 - Refers to Prince Ying, a member of the imperial family.
  • 开成末 - The late period of the Kai Cheng era, during which Emperor Wenzong ruled.
  • 李德裕 - A prominent chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 宣宗 - Refers to Emperor Xuangong, who succeeded Wuzong.
  • 端陵 - The tomb of Emperor Wenzong, located in the pine city of Duanling.
  • 方士 - Refers to alchemists or Daoist practitioners who claimed to have knowledge of immortality.
  • 饵药长年 - Refers to the practice of consuming elixirs to achieve longevity or immortality.
  • 才人 - A rank of concubine in the imperial harem.
  • 贤妃 - A posthumous title given to a virtuous concubine.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后生三子，皆秀嶷 - Describes the birth of three children who are all exceptionally talented and virtuous.
  • 其季仲宣，标字清峻 - Describes the youngest son, Zhongxuan, as having a noble and refined character.
  • 后既病，仲宣甫四岁，保育于别院 - The queen was already ill, and her youngest son, Zhongxuan, who was just four years old, was cared for in a separate courtyard.
  • 忽遘暴疾，数日卒 - Zhongxuan suddenly contracted a severe illness and died within a few days.
  • 后闻之，哀号颠仆，遂致大渐 - The queen heard of her son's death, screamed in grief, collapsed, and thus fell gravely ill.
  • 后虽病亟，爽迈如常 - Although the queen was gravely ill, she remained as lively and vigorous as usual.
  • 谓后主曰 - The queen told the emperor directly.
  • 婢子多幸，托质君门，冒宠乘华，凡十载矣 - She reflected on her life, expressing gratitude for being entrusted to the imperial household and enjoying favor and luxury for ten years.
  • 女子之荣，莫过于此 - She emphasized that the honor of a woman, being in the imperial household, is unparalleled.
  • 所不足者，子殇身殁，无以报德 - She expressed regret for the loss of her son and her inability to repay her husband's kindness.
  • 遂以元宗所赐琵琶及常臂玉环，亲遗后主 - She gave the pipa and jade bracelet bestowed by the empress dowager to the emperor personally.
  • 又自为书，请薄葬 - She wrote a letter requesting a simple burial.
  • 越三日，沐浴正衣妆，自内含玉，殂于瑶光殿之西室 - Three days later, she bathed, dressed, adorned herself, placed a jade bead in her mouth, and passed away in the western chamber of the Yao Guang Palace.
  • 时乾德二年十二月甲戌也，享年二十有九 - It was on the day Jia Xu of the twelfth month in the second year of Qiande, and she was twenty-nine years old at the time of her death.
  • 明年正月王午，迁灵柩于园寝 - In the fifth month of the following year, her coffin was moved to the mausoleum.
  • 后主哀苦，骨立，杖立而后起 - The emperor was deeply grieved, emaciated, and could only stand with a cane.

  • 后 - Refers to a queen or empress in the context of a royal court.
  • 后主 - Refers to the emperor, who is the husband or son of the queen.

  • 园寝 - Refers to a mausoleum or burial ground for the imperial family.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自为诔曰 - I compose a lament to say; '诔' (lěi) refers to a formal eulogy or lamentation.
  • 嗟嗟蒸民 - '嗟嗟' is an interjection expressing sorrow or lamentation; '蒸民' means common people or ordinary folk.
  • 嗜欲既胜 - '嗜欲' refers to desires or appetites; '既胜' means have already triumphed or have already prevailed.
  • 悲叹纠纷 - '悲叹' means grieve or lament; '纠纷' means disputes or conflicts.
  • 缘情攸宅 - '缘情' means following one's feelings; '攸宅' means dwelling or settling.
  • 触事来津 - '触事' means encountering matters or facing events; '来津' means to come to a halt or to stop.
  • 赀盈世逸 - '赀盈' means wealthy or abundant wealth; '世逸' means worldly ease or ease in life.
  • 乐鲜愁殷 - '乐鲜' means rare joy or scarcely any joy; '愁殷' means deep sorrow or abundant sorrow.
  • 沉乌逞兔 - '沉乌' refers to the sun setting; '逞兔' refers to the moon rising, symbolizing the passage of time.
  • 茂夏凋春 - '茂夏' means luxuriant summer; '凋春' means withered spring, symbolizing the changing seasons.
  • 年弥念旷 - '年弥' means year after year; '念旷' means longing or yearning.
  • 得故亡新 - '得故' means to obtain the old; '亡新' means to lose the new.
  • 阙景颓岸 - '阙景' means fading scenery; '颓岸' means collapsing cliffs or decaying banks.
  • 世阅川奔 - '世阅' means the world passing by; '川奔' means rushing rivers.
  • 外物交感 - '外物' means external things; '交感' means interacting or affecting each other.
  • 诡梦高唐 - '诡梦' means strange dreams; '高唐' refers to a place in ancient China, often associated with romantic or mythical tales.
  • 诞夸洛浦 - '诞夸' means exaggerated praise; '洛浦' refers to the Luo River, often associated with beauty and romance.
  • 曲平虚 - '曲平' means bending or curving; '虚' means empty or void.
  • 亦悯终古 - '亦悯' means also mourn; '终古' means throughout ancient times.
  • 况我心摧 - '况我' means as for me; '心摧' means heartbroken.
  • 兴哀有地 - '兴哀' means to arouse sorrow; '有地' means has a place or has a reason.
  • 苍苍何辜 - '苍苍' means vast or boundless; '何辜' means what wrong or what sin.
  • 歼予伉俪 - '歼予' means destroy me; '伉俪' means spouse or partner.
  • 窈窕难追 - '窈窕' means graceful or elegant; '难追' means hard to follow or hard to catch up with.
  • 不禄于世 - '不禄' means not to receive a salary or to die; '于世' means in the world.
  • 玉润珠融 - '玉润' means jade-like smoothness; '珠融' means pearl-like fusion.
  • 殒然破碎 - '殒然' means falling down; '破碎' means crushed or broken.
  • 柔仪俊德 - '柔仪' means gentle appearance; '俊德' means excellent virtue.
  • 孤映双纤 - '孤映' means single reflection; '双纤' means double delicacy.
  • 鲜挺秀 - '鲜' means fresh and bright; '挺秀' means standing out or emerging prominently.
  • 婉娈开扬 - '婉娈' means graceful and charming; '开扬' means open and expansive.
  • 艳不至冶 - '艳' means beautiful; '不至冶' means not overly seductive.
  • 慧或亡伤 - '慧' means wise; '亡伤' means not causing harm.
  • 盘迪奚诫 - '盘迪' means to guide; '奚诫' means what warning.
  • 慎肃惟常 - '慎肃' means cautious and solemn; '惟常' means only constant.
  • 佩环爱节 - '佩环' means ornaments worn on the body; '爱节' means loving the rhythm.
  • 造次有章 - '造次' means hastily; '有章' means with rules or with order.
  • 含颦发笑 - '含颦' means smiling with a slight frown; '发笑' means laughing.
  • 擢秀胜芳 - '擢秀' means standing out; '胜芳' means exceeding fragrance.
  • 鬓云留鉴 - '鬓云' means hair like clouds; '留鉴' means leaving a reflection.
  • 眼彩飞光 - '眼彩' means eye color; '飞光' means flying light or shining.
  • 情澜春媚 - '情澜' means emotional waves; '春媚' means spring charm.
  • 爱语风香 - '爱语' means loving words; '风香' means fragrant wind.
  • 瑰姿禀异 - '瑰姿' means beautiful form; '禀异' means endowed with uniqueness.
  • 金冶昭样 - '金冶' means gold refining; '昭样' means bright pattern.
  • 娩容亡犯 - '娩容' means gentle appearance; '亡犯' means not offending.
  • 均教多方 - '均教' means even teaching; '多方' means many ways.
  • 茫茫独逝 - '茫茫' means vast and boundless; '独逝' means alone departing.
  • 舍我何乡 - '舍我' means leaving me; '何乡' means where to go.
  • 昔我新婚 - '昔我' means in the past, I; '新婚' means newly married.
  • 燕尔情好 - '燕尔' means happy and content; '情好' means love and affection.
  • 媒亡劳辞 - '媒' means matchmaker; '亡' means no; '劳辞' means troublesome words.
  • 筮亡违报 - '筮' means divination; '亡' means no; '违报' means contrary report.
  • 归妹邀终 - '归妹' means returning sister; '邀' means invite; '终' means end.
  • 咸交协兆 - '咸交' means all together; '协兆' means harmonious signs.
  • 俯仰同心 - '俯仰' means up and down; '同心' means same heart.
  • 绸缪是道 - '绸缪' means meticulous; '是道' means this way.
  • 执子之手，与子偕老 - '执子之手' means hold your hand; '与子偕老' means grow old together.
  • 今也如何，不终往告 - '今也如何' means now what; '不终往告' means not to the end, not to inform.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 诔 - A formal eulogy or lamentation, often used in classical Chinese literature to mourn the dead.
  • 高唐 - A place in ancient China, often associated with romantic or mythical tales, particularly in the context of the *Shan Hai Jing* (Classic of Mountains and Seas).
  • 洛浦 - The Luo River, often associated with beauty and romance, especially in the context of the *Shan Hai Jing* (Classic of Mountains and Seas).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 每于花朝月夕 - Poetic phrase meaning 'at every flower festival and moonlit night,' referring to special occasions when people traditionally celebrate nature.
  • 无不伤怀 - Means 'one cannot help but feel sorrowful,' expressing deep emotional distress.
  • 凭栏惆怅 - Means 'leaning on the railing with a sense of melancholy,' describing a state of contemplation and sadness.
  • 不觉然泪眼低 - Means 'unconsciously lowering one's tearful eyes,' depicting a moment of emotional release.
  • 层城亡复见娇姿 - Means 'the layered city no longer sees its beautiful form,' lamenting the loss of something once beautiful.
  • 佳节缠哀不自持 - Means 'during the festival, one is overwhelmed by grief and cannot control oneself,' emphasizing the intensity of sorrow during festive times.
  • 空有当年旧烟月 - Means 'only the old smoke and moon from the past remain,' suggesting a sense of nostalgia and loss.
  • 芙蓉池上哭蛾眉 - Means 'crying over the painted eyebrows by the lotus pond,' referring to a woman's tears over the beauty that has faded.
  • 皆因后作 - Means 'all due to subsequent works,' implying that the sorrow is caused by later events or creations.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花朝 - Refers to the traditional Chinese festival celebrating the arrival of spring and the blooming of flowers.
  • 月夕 - Refers to the Mid-Autumn Festival, a time for family reunions and moon gazing.
  • 层城 - A poetic term for a city or palace, often used to describe a grand and layered urban landscape.
  • 芙蓉池 - Refers to a lotus pond, symbolizing purity and beauty in Chinese culture.
  • 蛾眉 - Refers to the arched eyebrows of a beautiful woman, often used as a metaphor for female beauty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '每于花朝月夕，无不伤怀' sets the tone for the entire passage, indicating that the speaker experiences profound sorrow during these special occasions.
  • The clause '凭栏惆怅人谁会' suggests a sense of isolation and the difficulty of finding someone to share one's feelings with.
  • The line '层城亡复见娇姿' laments the loss of something once beautiful, possibly a person or a place.
  • The sentence '佳节缠哀不自持' emphasizes the overwhelming nature of grief during festive times, making it difficult to maintain composure.
  • The line '空有当年旧烟月' reflects on the past and the changes that have occurred, suggesting a sense of nostalgia and loss.
  • The final line '芙蓉池上哭蛾眉' combines imagery of a lotus pond and a woman's tears, symbolizing the fading of beauty and the passage of time.
  • The concluding phrase '皆因后作' ties the entire passage together, suggesting that the sorrow is a result of subsequent events or creations.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 衔壁牵羊例击旗 - This phrase is a metaphorical description of the humiliation of surrender, but it can be read as a series of actions performed by the surrendering ruler.
  • 二十万军高拱手 - The phrase is elliptical and can be interpreted as either the soldiers raising their hands in submission or the ruler himself making a gesture of submission.
  • 更无一个是男儿 - The rhetorical question implies that none of the soldiers exhibited the qualities of a true man, but it can be read as a broader critique of the entire military leadership.
  • 有蜀僧远公有《伤废国》诗曰 - The phrase introduces a poem by a monk, but the exact relationship between the monk and the events described is not clear.
  • 乐极悲来数有涯 - The phrase suggests that joy is followed by sorrow, but the exact nature of the sorrow is not specified.
  • 歌声才歇便兴嗟 - The phrase describes a transition from joy to sorrow, but the exact cause of the sorrow is not clear.
  • 牵羊废主寻倾国 - The phrase describes the surrender of the ruler and the subsequent downfall of the nation, but the exact sequence of events is not specified.
  • 指鹿奸臣尽破家 - The phrase uses a metaphor to describe the actions of corrupt officials, but the exact consequences of their actions are not clear.
  • 丹禁夜凉空锁月 - The phrase describes a scene in the imperial palace, but the exact emotional tone is not clear.
  • 后庭春暖谩开花 - The phrase describes a scene in the imperial court, but the exact emotional tone is not clear.
  • 两朝帝业空成梦 - The phrase suggests that the imperial rule of two dynasties was ultimately futile, but the exact reasons for this are not specified.
  • 陵树苍苍噪暮鸦 - The phrase describes a scene at a tomb, but the exact emotional tone is not clear.

  • 固请不已 - Persistently requesting or insisting repeatedly.
  • 再拜就坐 - Bowing twice and then taking a seat.

  • 与其醉声色，何如与学士论文 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is it better to be drunk on music and women or to discuss literature with a scholar?'

  • 中秋有月 - Mid-Autumn Festival, a significant traditional Chinese holiday celebrated with family gatherings, moon gazing, and the sharing of mooncakes.
  • 谢庄赋 - Literary work by Xie Zhuang, a famous poet of the Southern Dynasties.
  • 李白诗 - Poetry by Li Bai, one of the greatest poets of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 御制诗 - Poetry composed by the emperor himself.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夜漏三鼓 - Refers to the third watch of the night, approximately midnight, using the ancient Chinese timekeeping system.
  • 令左右宫嫔各取领中裙带、或团扇手帕求诗 - A command for the emperor's attendants and concubines to bring their sashes, fans, handkerchiefs, or other items for poetry composition.
  • 内侍举牙床以金镶水晶砚、珊瑚笔、格玉管笔，皆上所用者于公前 - The eunuchs bring out a writing desk with a gold-inlaid crystal inkstone, coral pen, and jade-tube pen, all items used by the emperor, to the scholar's presence.
  • 来者应之，略不停缀 - The scholar responds immediately and without pause, indicating his quick wit and skill.
  • 都不蹈袭前人，尽出一时新意 - The poems are entirely original and do not imitate previous works, showcasing the scholar's creativity.
  • 仍称其所长，如美貌者，必及其容色 - The poems praise the women's virtues, with beautiful women being described in terms of their appearance.
  • 人人得其欢心，悉以进呈 - The poems please everyone, and they are all presented to the emperor.
  • 岂可虚辱，须与学士润笔 - The emperor says it would be inappropriate to leave the scholar unrewarded, so he should receive a gift.
  • 遂各取头上珠花一朵，装公幞头，簪不尽者，置公服袖中 - The women each take a flower from their hair and place it on the scholar's headband, with any left over being placed in his sleeves.
  • 宫人旋取针线缝联袖口 - The maidservants quickly sew the scholar's sleeves together to keep the flowers in place.
  • 宴罢，月将西沉，上命辄金莲烛，令内侍扶掖归院 - After the banquet, as the moon sets, the emperor orders the lighting of golden lotus lamps and has eunuchs escort the scholar back to his quarters.
  • 翌日，问：“学士夜来醉否？” - The next day, the emperor asks if the scholar was drunk the previous night.
  • 奏云，“虽有酒不醉。到玉堂，不解带便上床，取幞头在面前，抱两公服袖坐睡，恐失花也。” - The scholar replies that although he drank, he did not get drunk. Upon arriving at the Jade Hall, he immediately went to bed without untying his headband, holding the sleeves of his official robes to keep the flowers in place while sleeping.
  • 都下盛传天子请客 - The event becomes widely known throughout the capital, with the emperor being seen as hosting a grand banquet.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夜漏三鼓 - Refers to the ancient Chinese timekeeping system, where the night was divided into five watches, each lasting about two hours.
  • 宫嫔 - Refers to the emperor's concubines and attendants.
  • 金镶水晶砚、珊瑚笔、格玉管笔 - These are luxurious writing implements, symbolizing the emperor's wealth and the high regard for the scholar.
  • 玉堂 - Refers to the scholar's residence or study, often associated with high-ranking officials.
  • 金莲烛 - Golden lotus lamps, symbolizing luxury and the emperor's generosity.
  • 都下盛传天子请客 - The event is widely talked about in the capital, indicating the emperor's popularity and the scholar's reputation.

Proper Nouns

  • 金莲烛 - Refers to the golden lotus lamps.

  • 医曹孝忠侍疾无状，阁内侍王尧臣坐盗金珠，及出金明池游宴事 - Means 'the physician Cao Xiaozhong failed to properly attend to the empress, and the eunuch Wang Yaocen was imprisoned for stealing gold and jewels and for organizing a banquet at Jinming Pool.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening rhetorical comparison argues from the greater case to the lesser one.
  • The clause is elliptical but means that examples of this principle are found throughout the classics and histories.
  • The closing sequence compresses concession, limitation, and purpose into a single clause.

[482]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 皇帝幸鳴鸞堂 - The phrase '幸' here means 'to visit' or 'to grace with a visit,' often used in the context of the emperor visiting a place.
  • 淑妃從 - '淑妃' refers to a noblewoman or concubine, and '從' means 'to accompany.'
  • 臣京朝堂下，移班拜妃 - '移班' means to move or change position, and '拜妃' means to pay respects to the noblewoman.
  • 內侍連呼曰：“妃答拜。” - '內侍' refers to eunuchs or attendants, and '連呼曰' means 'continuously called out.'
  • 臣頓首曰 - '頓首' is a formal bow, often used in official settings.
  • 昔人三顧堂，成已六幸，千載榮遇 - '三顧堂' refers to the Three Visits to the Thatched Cottage, a famous historical event involving Liu Bei and Zhuge Liang. '六幸' means 'six visits,' and '千載榮遇' means 'a great honor for a thousand years.'
  • 為卿从容 - '为卿从容' means 'to take it easy for you' or 'to relax for you.'
  • 退西庑视庖膳 - '退西庑' means 'to retreat to the west wing,' and '视庖膳' means 'to inspect the food.'
  • 遣使持玛瑙大赐酒 - '遣使' means 'to send an envoy,' and '持玛瑙大赐酒' means 'to bring a large amount of wine in a jade vessel.'
  • 西亲手调茶，分赐左右 - '亲手调茶' means 'to personally prepare tea,' and '分赐左右' means 'to distribute it to those around.'
  • 妃小酌，遣赐道由臣堂视卧内，嗟其弊恶 - '小酌' means 'to take a small drink,' and '嗟其弊恶' means 'to lament its dilapidated state.'
  • 步至芝所，上立门屏侧语臣曰 - '步至芝所' means 'to walk to where the fungus was,' and '立门屏侧语臣曰' means 'stood beside the door and spoke to me.'
  • 不御袍带，不可相见，可去冠服 - '不御袍带' means 'without wearing a robe and sash,' and '不可相见' means 'cannot meet.' '可去冠服' means 'you can remove your hat and robe.'
  • 臣惶怖曰 - '惶怖' means 'frightened and terrified.'
  • 既为姻家，置君臣礼，当叙亲 - '既为姻家' means 'since we are related by marriage,' '置君臣礼' means 'set aside the formalities of ruler and subject,' and '当叙亲' means 'we should talk like family.'
  • 上亲酌，手持橄榄以赐 - '亲酌' means 'to personally pour,' and '手持橄榄以赐' means 'held an olive to give.'

  • 橄榄 - Olives, often used as a symbol of peace or goodwill in Chinese culture.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 道家金柜玉笈之书 - Refers to Daoist texts stored in golden cabinets and jade chests, symbolizing the sacred and valuable nature of these texts.
  • 神霄诸天隐文 - Refers to esoteric texts from the 'Heavenly Palace of the Divine Thunder' (神霄), a Daoist celestial realm. '隐文' means 'hidden writings' or 'secret texts.'
  • 稽古阁 - A name for a pavilion or hall dedicated to studying ancient texts or history. '稽古' means 'to study ancient texts' or 'to investigate ancient matters.'
  • 宣王石鼓 - Refers to stone drums inscribed with texts from the reign of King Xuan of Zhou, a historical artifact.
  • 历邃古、尚古、鉴古、作古、传古、博古、秘古诸阁 - A series of halls or pavilions named after their functions related to ancient texts: '邃古' (deep antiquity), '尚古' (valuing antiquity), '鉴古' (reflecting on antiquity), '作古' (creating antiquity), '传古' (transmitting antiquity), '博古' (broad antiquity), and '秘古' (secret antiquity).
  • 祖宗训谟 - Refers to the teachings and edicts of ancestors, likely the founding emperors of a dynasty.
  • 夏商周尊彝鼎鬲爵卤敦盘盂 - Refers to ritual vessels from the Shang, Zhou, and Xia dynasties, including bronze vessels like tripods, jars, and wine vessels. '尊' (wine vessels), '彝' (bronze vessels), '鼎' (tripods), '鬲' (cauldrons), '爵' (wine cups), '卤' (salt containers), '敦' (food containers), '盘' (bowls), and '盂' (baskets).
  • 汉晋隋唐书画 - Refers to paintings and calligraphy from the Han, Jin, Sui, and Tang dynasties.
  • 震悸惶怖 - A compound term meaning 'shocked, trembling, and terrified,' expressing a state of extreme fear.
  • 乳花盈面 - Refers to frothy milk spilling over the face, likely from a tea ceremony or offering. '乳花' is a poetic term for the foam on tea.
  • 陛下略君臣夷等 - A phrase meaning 'Your Majesty treats ministers and subjects equally,' indicating a relaxed and informal relationship between the ruler and his subjects.
  • 可少休 - A polite request meaning 'please take a short rest.'
  • 宝林殿 - The name of a palace or hall, likely a place of importance in the imperial complex.
  • 留题殿壁 - Means 'to inscribe or leave a message on the palace wall,' a common practice in imperial settings for commemoration or instruction.
  • 喻臣笔墨已具 - A phrase meaning 'Your Majesty informs me that my writing materials are ready,' indicating preparation for an inscription or writing task.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道家金柜玉笈之书 - Refers to Daoist texts, which were often stored in elaborate containers to signify their sacredness. Daoism was a major philosophical and religious tradition in China, influencing imperial culture and rituals.
  • 神霄诸天隐文 - Refers to esoteric Daoist texts associated with the 'Heavenly Palace of the Divine Thunder' (神霄), a celestial realm in Daoist cosmology. These texts were often kept secret and passed down through initiates.
  • 宣王石鼓 - Refers to stone drums inscribed with texts from the reign of King Xuan of Zhou (r. 827–782 BCE), a significant historical artifact that provides insight into early Chinese writing and governance.
  • 祖宗训谟 - Refers to the teachings and edicts of the founding emperors of a dynasty, emphasizing the importance of tradition and continuity in imperial rule.
  • 夏商周尊彝鼎鬲爵卤敦盘盂 - Refers to ritual vessels from the Shang, Zhou, and Xia dynasties, which were used in ceremonies and symbolized the power and legitimacy of the ruling class.
  • 汉晋隋唐书画 - Refers to the artistic achievements of the Han, Jin, Sui, and Tang dynasties, which are considered golden ages of Chinese art and culture.
  • 陛下略君臣夷等 - Reflects a moment of informal interaction between the emperor and his subjects, highlighting the emperor's benevolence and approachability.
  • 乳花盈面 - Refers to the frothy milk from a tea ceremony, a common practice in imperial settings. Tea was an important part of Chinese culture and often symbolized hospitality and respect.

  • 有才兼文武似我者乎 - Means 'is there anyone who has both literary and martial talents like me?' '才' means 'talent' or 'ability,' and '文武' refers to literary and martial skills.
  • 有豐富偉岸过于我者乎 - Means 'is there anyone who is more wealthy and impressive than me?' '豐富' means 'wealthy' or 'abundant,' '偉岸' means 'impressive' or 'majestic,' and '过于' means 'more than.'

  • 始，偃与母以卖珠为事 - "始" means 'originally,' and '卖珠' means 'selling pearls.' This phrase indicates that Dong Yan and his mother originally made a living by selling pearls.
  • 随母出入主家 - "随母" means 'followed his mother,' and '主家' refers to the princess's household. This phrase indicates that Dong Yan followed his mother into the princess's household.
  • 左右言其姣好 - "左右" means 'those around her,' and '姣好' means 'beautiful.' This phrase indicates that those around the princess praised Dong Yan's beauty.
  • 主召见曰 - "主" refers to the princess, and '召见' means 'called to see.' This phrase indicates that the princess summoned Dong Yan to see her.
  • 吾为母养之 - "吾" means 'I,' and '养之' means 'raise him.' This phrase indicates that the princess offered to raise Dong Yan.
  • 因留第中 - "因" means 'therefore,' and '第中' means 'in the palace.' This phrase indicates that Dong Yan was therefore kept in the palace.
  • 教书计、相马、御、射 - "书计" means 'writing and arithmetic,' '相马' means 'horse breeding,' '御' means 'charioteering,' and '射' means 'archery.' This phrase indicates that he was taught these skills.
  • 颇读传记 - "颇" means 'quite a bit,' and '传记' means 'biographies.' This phrase indicates that he read a considerable amount of biographies.
  • 至年十八而冠 - "至" means 'until,' '年十八' means 'eighteen years old,' and '冠' means 'came of age.' This phrase indicates that he came of age at eighteen.
  • 出则执辔，入则侍内 - "出则" means 'when going out,' '执辔' means 'holding the reins,' '入则' means 'when coming in,' and '侍内' means 'serving inside.' This phrase indicates that he served both as a charioteer and an attendant.
  • 为人温柔爱人 - "为人" means 'personality,' '温柔' means 'gentle,' and '爱人' means 'loves people.' This phrase indicates that he was gentle and loving.
  • 以主故，诸公接之 - "以主故" means 'because of the princess,' and '诸公' means 'various nobles.' This phrase indicates that various nobles befriended him because of the princess.
  • 名称城中 - "名" means 'fame,' and '城中' means 'in the city.' This phrase indicates that he became famous in the city.
  • 号曰董君 - "号曰" means 'called,' and '董君' is his nickname. This phrase indicates that he was called Dong Jun.
  • 主因推令散财交士 - "主因" means 'the princess therefore,' '推令' means 'commanded,' '散财' means 'distribute wealth,' and '交士' means 'befriend scholars.' This phrase indicates that the princess commanded him to distribute wealth and befriend scholars.
  • 令中府曰 - "令中府" means 'ordered the treasury,' and '曰' means 'said.' This phrase indicates that he was instructed to say the following.
  • 董君所发，一日金满百斤，钱满百万，帛满千匹，乃白之 - "董君所发" means 'what Dong Jun distributes,' '一日' means 'one day,' '金满百斤' means 'gold worth a hundred pounds,' '钱满百万' means 'cash worth a million,' and '帛满千匹' means 'silk worth a thousand bolts.' This phrase indicates that he was instructed to distribute wealth until it reached these amounts before reporting to the princess.

  • 董君绿帻傅鞴，随主前，伏殿下 - Describes Dong Jun's appearance and actions, meaning 'Dong Jun wore a green headband and followed the princess to the hall, bowing before the emperor.'

  • 奈何乎陛下 - Rhetorical question meaning 'what should be done, Your Majesty?'

  • 不可！夫宣室者，先帝之正处也，非法度之政，不得入焉 - '夫宣室者，先帝之正处也' means 'the Xuan Room is the proper place of the late emperor,' and '非法度之政，不得入焉' means 'if it is not in accordance with the laws and regulations, one cannot enter.'
  • 放淫乱之渐，其变为篡 - '放淫乱之渐' means 'allowing the gradual spread of debauchery,' and '其变为篡' means 'which will eventually lead to usurpation.'
  • 是以竖貂为淫，而易牙作患 - '是以竖貂为淫' means 'thus, the vertical pig (a metaphor for debauchery) became debauched,' and '而易牙作患' means 'and Yi Ya caused trouble.'
  • 庆父诛，而鲁国全；管蔡诛，而周室安 - '庆父诛，而鲁国全' means 'when Qingfu was executed, the state of Lu was preserved,' and '管蔡诛，而周室安' means 'when Guan and Cai were executed, the Zhou dynasty was secured.'
  • 善 - Simple affirmative, meaning 'good' or 'well said.'
  • 有诏止，更置酒北宫，引董君从东司马门 - '有诏止' means 'an edict was issued to stop,' '更置酒北宫' means 'and to set up a banquet in the northern palace,' and '引董君从东司马门' means 'and to lead Lady Dong from the eastern military gate.'
  • 东司马门更名东交门 - Means 'the eastern military gate was renamed the eastern exchange gate.'
  • 赐朔黄金三十斤 - Means 'a grant of thirty catties of gold was given to Shuo.'
  • 董君之宠，由是日衰，至年三十而终 - '董君之宠，由是日衰' means 'Lady Dong's favor began to decline,' and '至年三十而终' means 'until she died at the age of thirty.'
  • 后数岁，窦太主卒，与董君会葬于霸陵 - '后数岁' means 'several years later,' '窦太主卒' means 'Dowager Princess Dou died,' and '与董君会葬于霸陵' means 'and Lady Dong was buried with her at Baling.'
  • 是后公主贵人多逾礼制，自董偃始 - '是后公主贵人多逾礼制' means 'after this, many princesses and noblewomen exceeded the proper etiquette,' and '自董偃始' means 'starting with Dong Yan.'
