  • 以画石为床，文如锦绣，石质甚轻，出郅支国 - With painted stone as a bed, patterned like brocade, the stone is very light, from the country of Zhizhi. This suggests a luxurious or artistic bed made of stone.
  • 上设紫琉璃帐，火齐屏凤，列灵麻之烛，以紫玉为盘，如屈龙，皆用杂宝饰之 - The emperor set up a purple glass tent, fire-screen phoenixes, arranged with lingma candles, with purple jade as a tray, like a coiled dragon, all decorated with various treasures. This indicates the use of multiple precious materials.
  • 侍者于户外扇偃，偃曰：“玉石岂须扇而后凉耶！”侍者乃却扇，以手摸，方知有屏风 - The attendant fanned Yan outside, Yan said, 'Do jade and stone need to be fanned to be cool?' Then she withdrew the fan and touched it with her hand, only then did she realize there was a screen. This indicates a physical interaction.
  • 又以玉精为盘，贮冰于膝前，玉精与冰同其洁澈 - Again, with jade essence as a tray, placed ice in front of the knees, the jade essence and ice are equally pure and clear. This indicates the use of ice for cooling.
  • 公须髯如戟，何无丈夫意 - Your beard is like a halberd, why do you lack the spirit of a man? This is a rhetorical question implying a lack of masculinity or courage.
  • 以死自誓，乃得免 - Swore by his death, and was thus spared. This indicates the outcome of the oath.
  • 山阴公主，宋武帝女，废帝妹也。适何戢 - Princess Shanyin, daughter of Emperor Wu of Song, sister of the deposed emperor, married to He Ji. This provides the historical context.
  • 何戢少美丽，动止与褚渊相慕，时号为小褚 - He Ji was young and beautiful, his movements and stops were admired by Zhu Yuan, at the time, he was called 'Little Zhu.' This indicates a nickname.
  • 帝为置面首三十人，褚渊亦与焉，主尤慕爱之 - The emperor arranged for thirty face companions, Zhu Yuan was also among them, the princess especially admired and loved him. This indicates the emperor's response.
  • 渊以死自誓，乃得免 - Zhu Yuan swore by his death, and was thus spared. This indicates the outcome of his oath.

  • 風流蕴藉 - Describes someone as elegant and refined.
  • 舉登第 - Refers to passing the imperial examinations, a significant achievement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 公主 - Refers to a princess, likely a member of the imperial family.
  • 岐王 - Refers to Prince Qi, a nobleman who is likely a patron of the arts.
  • 張九皋 - Likely a high-ranking official or scholar, recommended by the princess.
  • 京兆 - Refers to the capital region, specifically the area governed by the governor of Jingzhao.
  • 解头 - Refers to the top candidate in the imperial examinations.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 最幼女 - Means 'youngest daughter.'
  • 解衣以褓之，名曰裹兒 - Describes the emperor wrapping the newborn in his own clothes and naming her 'Gua'er' (Wrapped Child).
  • 姝秀辩敏 - Describes the princess as beautiful, graceful, eloquent, and intelligent.
  • 下嫁武崇训 - Means 'married down to Wu Chongxun,' implying a marriage to a man of lower status.
  • 尝作诏请帝署可，帝笑而从之 - Describes the princess writing an edict and having the emperor sign it, with the emperor smiling and agreeing.
  • 又请为皇太女，右仆射魏元忠谏 - The princess requested to be made the Crown Princess, and Chancellor Wei Yuanzhong advised against it.
  • 侯王柄臣，多出其门 - Indicates that many nobles and powerful officials came from her connections.
  • 皆出屠贩，纳赀售官，降墨敕斜封授之 - Explains that these officials were often from lowly backgrounds, purchased their positions, and were appointed through unofficial, slanted seals.
  • 主营第，及安乐佛庐，皆宪写宫省，而工致过之 - Describes the princess's main residence and her Buddhist temple, which were modeled after the imperial palaces but with even greater craftsmanship.
  • 尝请昆明池为私沼 - The princess requested to convert the Imperial Lake of Kunming into her private lake.
  • 主不悦，自凿定昆池，延袤数里 - The princess was displeased and had her own lake, Diding Pool, excavated, which extended for several miles.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 安乐公主 - A historical figure known for her beauty and influence during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 帝迁房陵 - Refers to Emperor Zhongzong's exile in Fangling, a significant event in Tang history.
  • 武崇训 - A member of the powerful Wu clan, known for his role in the Tang court.
  • 魏元忠 - A chancellor known for his opposition to the Wu clan's influence.
  • 阿武子 - Refers to Wu Zetian, the Empress Regnant of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 昆明池 - The Imperial Lake of Kunming, a significant imperial site.
  • 定昆池 - The princess's private lake, named after the imperial lake but with a different spelling.

Proper Nouns

  • 安乐公主 - The name of the princess, which will remain unchanged in English.
  • 帝 - Refers to Emperor Zhongzong.
  • 武崇训 - The name of the princess's husband.
  • 魏元忠 - The name of the chancellor.
  • 阿武子 - Refers to Wu Zetian.
  • 昆明池 - The name of the Imperial Lake of Kunming.
  • 定昆池 - The name of the princess's private lake.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 司农卿 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty responsible for state finances and granaries.
  • 缮沼累石肖华山 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'to build a pond, pile stones to resemble Mount Hua,' referring to the construction of an artificial landscape.
  • 回渊九折 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'a winding, deep, and multi-turn path,' often used to describe a complex or intricate design.
  • 石瀵水 - Literally 'water gushing from stone,' referring to water features or fountains.
  • 宝炉镂怪兽神禽 - Describes a furnace or incense burner adorned with carvings of mythical creatures and divine birds.
  • 间以珊瑚磲贝 - Means 'interspersed with corals, mother-of-pearl, and shells,' indicating the use of precious materials in decoration.
  • 不可涯计 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'beyond count or measure,' emphasizing the abundance or grandeur.
  • 崇训死，主改降武延秀 - A historical event where after the death of a certain individual (崇训), the princess (主) changed her mind and chose another suitor (武延秀).
  • 突厥舞 - Refers to a specific dance style from the Turkic people.
  • 貌韶秀 - Describes a person as 'graceful and handsome.'
  • 妖丽自喜 - Means 'feeling proud of one's beauty and charm.'
  • 数与内庭宴 - Means 'often entertained in the inner palace,' indicating frequent social gatherings.
  • 假后车 - Means 'borrowing the imperial carriage,' a sign of high status or favor.
  • 自宫送至第 - Means 'from the palace to her residence,' indicating a formal procession.
  • 帝与后为御安福门临观 - Means 'the emperor and empress viewed from the Anfu Gate,' indicating a royal audience.
  • 诏雍州长史窦怀贞为礼会使 - Means 'the emperor appointed Governor of Yongzhou, Dou Huazhen, as the chief official for the ceremony.'
  • 弘文学士为傧 - Means 'prominent scholars served as attendants,' indicating the importance of the event.
  • 相王障车捐赐金帛不赀 - Means 'Prince Xiang donated countless amounts of gold and silk to the vehicle,' indicating generous gifts.
  • 翌日，大会群臣太极殿 - Means 'the next day, a grand assembly of officials was held in the Taiji Hall,' indicating a significant political event.
  • 被翠服出 - Means 'dressed in green robes,' indicating a formal appearance.
  • 向天子再拜 - Means 'paid homage to the emperor twice,' indicating a formal ritual.
  • 南面拜公卿 - Means 'bowed to the ministers facing south,' indicating a formal greeting.
  • 公卿皆伏地稽首 - Means 'the ministers all prostrated themselves,' indicating a formal show of respect.
  • 武攸暨与太平公主偶舞，为帝寿 - Means 'Wu Youji and Princess Taiping danced together, offering a toast to the emperor,' indicating a celebratory performance.
  • 赐群臣帛数十万 - Means 'the emperor granted tens of thousands of bolts of silk to the officials,' indicating generous rewards.
  • 帝御承天门，大赦，因赐民三日 - Means 'the emperor ascended the Chengtian Gate, proclaimed a general amnesty, and granted the people three days off,' indicating a significant public announcement.
  • 内外官赐勋禄礼，官属兼阶爵 - Means 'officials inside and outside the court were granted ranks, titles, and honors,' indicating widespread rewards.
  • 夺临川长公主宅以为第 - Means 'the residence of Princess Linchuan was taken to serve as the new residence,' indicating a significant property transfer.
  • 旁彻民庐 - Means 'it extended to the homes of the people,' indicating the impact on the surrounding area.
  • 第成，野藏空殚 - Means 'when the residence was completed, the treasury was depleted,' indicating the cost of the project.
  • 假万骑仗内，音乐送主还第 - Means 'the imperial guards and musicians escorted the princess back to her new residence,' indicating a grand procession.
  • 天子亲幸宴 - Means 'the emperor personally attended the banquet,' indicating a high level of involvement.
  • 近臣崇训子方数岁，拜太常卿，封镐国公 - Means 'the young son of the close minister Chongxun was appointed as the Grand Master of Ceremonies and enfeoffed as Duke of Hao,' indicating a significant promotion.
  • 公主满孺月，帝后复幸第，大赦天下 - Means 'after the princess had her first month of confinement, the emperor and empress visited her residence again, and a general amnesty was proclaimed throughout the empire,' indicating a significant event.
  • 临淄王诛韦庶人 - Means 'Prince Linzi executed the commoner Wei Shuren,' indicating a political event.
  • 主方览镜画眉，闻乱，走至右延门，兵及而死 - Means 'the princess was looking into the mirror to apply makeup when she heard the news of the chaos, rushed to the Right Extension Gate, and was killed by the soldiers,' indicating a tragic end.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 司农卿 - A high-ranking official in the Tang dynasty responsible for state finances and granaries.

  • 镐国公 - A noble title, indicating a high rank in the nobility.
  • 临川长公主 - A historical figure, likely a princess who owned the residence taken for the new residence.
  • 承天门 - A gate in the imperial city, often used for significant public announcements.
  • 太极殿 - The main hall of the imperial palace, used for important ceremonies.
  • 安福门 - A gate in the imperial city, often used for important ceremonies.
  • 右延门 - A gate in the imperial city, likely used for important events.
  • 临淄王 - Refers to Li Longji, later Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, who played a significant role in the political upheaval.
  • 韦庶人 - Refers to Wei Shuren, a commoner who was executed by Prince Linzi.
  • 临淄王诛韦庶人 - A significant political event where Prince Linzi executed a commoner, reflecting the turbulent political climate.
  • 主方览镜画眉，闻乱，走至右延门，兵及而死 - A tragic event where the princess was killed by soldiers, reflecting the violence and chaos of the time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 紋布中，手中也 - Literal description of a type of fabric, '紋布' meaning 'patterned cloth,' and '手中也' meaning 'in hand' or 'used by hand.'
  • 潔白如雪，光軟，拭水不濡 - Series of descriptive phrases: '潔白如雪' means 'as white as snow,' '光軟' means 'smooth and soft,' and '拭水不濡' means 'does not become wet when wiped with water.'
  • 用之弥年，亦未尝生垢腻 - '用之弥年' means 'used for a long time,' and '亦未尝生垢腻' means 'still does not accumulate dirt or grease.'
  • 二物称得鬼谷国 - '二物' means 'two things' or 'two items,' and '称得鬼谷国' could mean 'are called from the country of Guiguzi' or 'are named after the country of Guiguzi.'
  • 火茧绵，云出火洲 - '火茧绵' means 'fire silk,' and '云出火洲' means 'said to come from the Fire Desert.'
  • 絮衣一袭，用之一两，稍过度，则蒸之气不可近 - '絮衣一袭' means 'a robe made of wool,' '用之一两' means 'using a small amount,' and '稍过度，则蒸之气不可近' means 'if used excessively, the heat becomes unbearable.'
  • 工巧妙丽，殆非人制 - '工巧妙丽' means 'exquisitely crafted and beautiful,' and '殆非人制' means 'probably not made by humans.'
  • 有得于金陵者，因以献公主，酬之甚厚 - '有得于金陵者' means 'someone from Jinling (Nanjing),' '因以献公主' means 'presented to the princess,' and '酬之甚厚' means 'received a generous reward.'
  • 一日昼寝，梦绛衣奴致语云 - '一日昼寝' means 'one day during the day,' '梦绛衣奴致语云' means 'dreamed of a red-clad servant saying.'
  • 南齐潘淑妃，取九鸾钗 - '南齐潘淑妃' means 'Pan Shufei of the Southern Qi Dynasty,' and '取九鸾钗' means 'took the nine phoenix hairpins.'
  • 及觉，具以梦中之言，言于左右 - '及觉' means 'upon waking,' '具以梦中之言' means 'reported the words from the dream,' and '言于左右' means 'to those around her.'
  • 公主薨，其钗亦亡其处 - '公主薨' means 'the princess died,' and '其钗亦亡其处' means 'the hairpins also disappeared.'
  • 韦氏异其事，遂以实话于门人 - '韦氏异其事' means 'Wei (the person's surname) found the matter strange,' and '遂以实话于门人' means 'and told the truth to his disciples.'
  • 逮诸珍异，不可具载 - '逮诸珍异' means 'including various rare and precious items,' and '不可具载' means 'cannot be fully listed.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鬼谷国 - A legendary place associated with the ancient Chinese philosopher Guiguzi (鬼谷子), known for his teachings on strategy and warfare.
  • 火洲 - A mythical place, possibly referring to a region known for its extreme heat.
  • 南齐潘淑妃 - Pan Shufei, a concubine of Emperor Wu of Southern Qi, known for her beauty and influence.
  • 九鸾钗 - Nine phoenix hairpins, a symbol of high status and beauty in ancient China.
  • 金陵 - The ancient name for Nanjing, a major city in southern China.

Proper Nouns

  • 鬼谷国 - The country of Guiguzi.
  • 火洲 - The Fire Desert.
  • 南齐潘淑妃 - Pan Shufei of the Southern Qi Dynasty.
  • 九鸾钗 - Nine phoenix hairpins.
  • 金陵 - Jinling (Nanjing).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 汉至唐，公主出降之盛，未之有也 - Formal statement meaning 'From the Han to the Tang, the grandeur of princesses being married off was unparalleled.'
  • 七宝步辇 - Refers to a luxurious carriage adorned with seven treasures, symbolizing wealth and status.
  • 五色玉香囊 - Describes a pouch made of jade in five colors, used to carry fragrances.
  • 辟邪香、瑞麝香、金凤香 - Names three types of fragrances:辟邪香 (辟邪香,辟邪香), 瑞麝香 (瑞麝香,瑞麝香), and 金凤香 (金凤香,金凤香). These are exotic scents, possibly from foreign lands.
  • 龙脑金屑 - Refers to a mixture of dragon's brain (a type of incense) and gold powder.
  • 络真珠玳瑁 - Refers to pearls and tortoiseshell used for decoration.
  • 金丝为流苏 - Describes tassels made of gold thread.
  • 雕轻玉为浮动 - Refers to delicate carvings on jade that appear to move.
  • 坐来香气何太异也 - A question meaning 'What is this unusual fragrance?'
  • 岂非龙脑耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'Is it not dragon's brain?'
  • 幼给事于嫔妃宫 - Means 'I served in the palace when I was young.'
  • 当垆者 - Refers to the person selling wine at the tavern.
  • 锦衣换酒于此 - Means 'exchanged fine clothes for wine here.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉至唐 - Refers to the period from the Han Dynasty to the Tang Dynasty, a time of significant cultural and political development in China.
  • 公主出降 - Refers to the marriage of a princess, a significant event in imperial courts.
  • 七宝步辇 - A symbol of imperial wealth and power.
  • 辟邪香、瑞麝香、金凤香 - These fragrances are likely from foreign lands, indicating the extensive trade and cultural exchanges during this period.
  • 龙脑金屑 - A luxury item, often used in religious and ceremonial contexts.
  • 络真珠玳瑁 - Pearls and tortoiseshell were highly valued materials in ancient China.
  • 金丝为流苏 - Tassels made of gold thread were a sign of luxury and refinement.
  • 雕轻玉为浮动 - Jade carvings were a symbol of high artistry and craftsmanship.
  • 中贵人 - Refers to a high-ranking official or courtier.
  • 广化旗亭 - A specific tavern or inn, possibly named after a place or a person.
  • 嫔妃宫 - Refers to the palace where the emperor's concubines lived.

Proper Nouns

  • 汉至唐 - The period from the Han to the Tang Dynasties.
  • 公主 - A princess, a member of the imperial family.
  • 广化旗亭 - A specific tavern or inn.
  • 中贵人 - A high-ranking official or courtier.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 消靈炙 - A type of dish, likely named for its special properties or ingredients. The exact nature is unclear but suggests a delicacy.
  • 紅虬脯 - A type of meat dish, possibly named for its appearance or preparation method. '虬' could mean a type of dragon or a specific kind of meat.
  • 凝露漿 - A type of drink, likely a clear, refreshing beverage.
  • 桂花醞 - A type of wine, possibly infused with osmanthus flowers.
  • 綠花紫英之號 - Refers to the names of two types of tea, '綠花' and '紫英,' which are likely descriptive of their appearance or quality.
  • 雖經暑毒，終不臭敗 - Despite being exposed to heat, the dish does not spoil or become foul-smelling.
  • 紅虬脯，非虬也，但呼於盤中，虬健如絲，高一丈，以箸抑之，無三數，分撒即復其故 - Describes the unusual properties of the '紅虬脯' dish, suggesting it has some magical or highly unusual characteristics.
  • 迫諸品味，人莫能識 - Despite its unique properties, people cannot fully understand or identify it.
  • 公主家人厭饫如里中糠秕 - The princess's family members are so accustomed to these delicacies that they find them as common as chaff in the village.
  • 一日，大會韋氏之族於廣化里 - One day, the princess held a grand gathering of the Wei family in Guanghua Lane.
  • 玉饌俱陳 - All the exquisite dishes were laid out.
  • 暑氣將甚 - The heat was intense.
  • 公主命取澄水，帛以蘸之，掛於南軒 - The princess ordered to bring clear water, soak a piece of silk in it, and hang it in the southern pavilion.
  • 澄水帛 - A piece of silk soaked in clear water, which has a cooling effect.
  • 其中有龍涎，故能消暑也 - It is said that the silk contains dragon's saliva, which gives it its cooling properties.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上 - The emperor, who is bestowing favors and gifts.
  • 公主 - The princess, who is hosting the gathering.
  • 韋氏之族 - The Wei family, a prominent clan in the story.
  • 廣化里 - A location where the gathering takes place.
  • 玉饌 - Exquisite dishes, often associated with royalty and high social status.
  • 澄水帛 - A piece of silk soaked in clear water, which is used to cool the room. This is a traditional Chinese method of cooling.
  • 龍涎 - Dragon's saliva, a mythical substance believed to have cooling properties.

Proper Nouns

  • 上 - The emperor.
  • 公主 - The princess.
  • 韋氏之族 - The Wei family.
  • 廣化里 - Guanghua Lane.
  • 澄水帛 - A piece of silk soaked in clear water.
  • 龍涎 - Dragon's saliva, a mythical substance believed to have cooling properties.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 争取灰以择金宝 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to sift through ashes to select gold and jewels,' used to describe the painstaking process of searching for valuable items.
  • 出内库金玉驼马 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to produce from the imperial treasury gold, jade, camels, and horses,' indicating the lavish display of wealth.
  • 凤凰麒麟 - Idiomatic expression referring to phoenixes and unicorns, mythical creatures symbolizing auspiciousness and nobility.
  • 不可胜计 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'too numerous to count,' emphasizing the vast quantity of items.
  • 焚升霄灵芝香 - Idiomatic expression describing the burning of fragrant lingzhi mushrooms to ascend to the heavens, a ritual act.
  • 击归天紫金之碧磬 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'to strike a green jade qing (a type of bell) made of purple gold to return to heaven,' another ritual act.
  • 流汗相属 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'sweating profusely and linking together,' describing the dense crowd.
  • 无不伤痛 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'everyone is deeply saddened,' expressing widespread grief.
  • 词质而意切 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'the words are simple but the meaning is sincere,' describing the emotional depth of the text.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 韦家 - Refers to the Wei family, likely a prominent family during the Tang dynasty.
  • 东郊 - Refers to the eastern suburbs of the capital city, often used for burial grounds.
  • 延兴门 - A gate in the capital city, likely the Tang capital Chang'an.
  • 上与淑妃 - Refers to the emperor and his favorite concubine, the Shufei.
  • 内库 - The imperial treasury, where the emperor's wealth is stored.
  • 紫尼及女道士 - Refers to purple-robed nuns and female Taoist priests, who were part of the religious and ceremonial entourage.
  • 灵卤 - Likely a misspelling of '灵柩' (lingjiu), meaning the coffin or bier.
  • 乳母 - Refers to a wet nurse or wetnurse, a common practice in ancient China to ensure the health of the royal offspring.

Proper Nouns

  • 韦家 - The Wei family.
  • 东郊 - The eastern suburbs of the capital city.
  • 延兴门 - A gate in the capital city.
  • 上 - The emperor.
  • 淑妃 - The Shufei, the emperor's favorite concubine.
  • 内库 - The imperial treasury.
  • 紫尼 - Purple-robed nuns.
  • 女道士 - Female Taoist priests.
  • 乳母 - A wet nurse or wetnurse.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上日夕惴心挂意 - The emperor was constantly worried and preoccupied, reflecting his deep concern.
  • 李可及叹追百年曲 - Li Keji lamented the 'Hundred Years' song, where 'Hundred Years' likely refers to a lament or ballad about long-term suffering or loss.
  • 声辞怨切 - The words and melody were filled with deep sorrow and resentment.
  • 听之莫不泪下 - Everyone who heard it could not help but shed tears.
  • 更教数千人，作叹百年队 - He further taught thousands of people to perform the 'Hundred Years' team, indicating a large-scale performance.
  • 取内库珍宝，雕成首饰 - He took treasures from the imperial treasury and crafted them into jewelry.
  • 画八百匹官绫作鱼龙波浪纹，以为地衣而舞 - He painted eight hundred pieces of official silk with dragon and wave patterns, using them as a dance floor.
  • 一舞珠翠满地 - After one dance, the floor was covered with jewels and precious stones.
  • 可及官历大将军，赏赐盈万 - Li Keji served as a general and received countless rewards.
  • 甚无状 - Extremely inappropriate or highly improper.
  • 左军容使西门季玄素梗直 - The left military commissioner Xi Men Ji Xuan was straightforward and honest.
  • 尔恣巧媚以惑天子，族无日矣 - You are using your charm to deceive the emperor; your family will not survive long.
  • 可及恃宠，未尝改作 - Li Keji relied on his favor and never changed his ways.
  • 可及善喉舌，于天子前弄眼作头脑 - Li Keji was skilled in speech and would make faces and gestures before the emperor.
  • 连声著词，唱杂声曲，须臾，则百数不休 - He would sing and compose songs continuously, and within a short time, he could produce many pieces.
  • 京城不调少年相效，谓之拍弹 - Young people in the capital imitated him, calling it 'paddle and hit.'
  • 一日，可及乞假，为子娶妇 - One day, Li Keji asked for leave to marry his son.
  • 上曰：“即令送酒面以助汝嘉礼。” - The emperor said, 'I will send wine and food to help you celebrate your son's wedding.'
  • 始谓之酒，及封启，皆实中也 - Initially, it was thought to be wine, but when opened, it was found to be solid.
  • 上赐可及银麒麟，高数尺 - The emperor granted Li Keji a silver qilin (a mythical creature), several feet tall.
  • 可及取官库车载归私第 - Li Keji had it transported from the official warehouse to his private residence.
  • 西门季玄曰：“今日受赐，吏用官车，它日破家，亦须辇还内府，不道受赏，徒劳牛足。” - Xi Men Ji Xuan said, 'Today you received a gift, but tomorrow if your family is ruined, you will have to return it to the imperial treasury. It's not worth the effort.'
  • 后果流可及于岭表 - Later, Li Keji was exiled to the southern regions.
  • 旧赐珍玩，悉皆进纳 - All the previous gifts and treasures were returned to the imperial treasury.
  • 君子谓季玄有先见 - The wise men said that Xi Men Ji Xuan had foresight.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 以冀恩封襄城君 - Means 'was enfeoffed as Lady of Xiangcheng due to Liang Ji's favor.' '封' means 'enfeoffed,' and '襄城君' is a noble title.
  • 兼食阳翟租，岁人五千万 - Means 'also received the rent from Yangdi, amounting to five million annually.' '兼食' means 'also receive,' '阳翟' is a place name, and '岁人' means 'annual income.'
  • 加赐赤级，比长公主 - Means 'was additionally granted a red rank, equivalent to that of a princess.' '加赐' means 'additionally granted,' '赤级' refers to a high-ranking official's attire, and '比长公主' means 'equivalent to a princess.'
  • 寿色美而善为妖态 - Means 'Sun Shou was beautiful and skilled in creating bewitching appearances.' '色美' means 'beautiful,' and '善为妖态' means 'skilled in creating bewitching appearances.'
  • 以为媚惑 - Means 'to charm and seduce.' '媚惑' means 'charm and seduce.'
  • 冀亦易舆服之制 - Means 'Liang Ji also changed the rules for carriages and clothing.' '易' means 'change,' and '舆服之制' refers to regulations regarding carriages and clothing.
  • 作平上车，埤帻狭冠，折上巾，拥身扇狐尾单衣 - Describes specific changes in attire: '平上车' means 'a flat-roofed carriage,' '埤帻狭冠' means 'a low hat with a narrow brim,' '折上巾' means 'a folded headband,' '拥身扇' means 'a fan held in front of the body,' and '狐尾单衣' means 'a single-layered robe with a fox tail.'
  • 寿性钳忌，能制御冀，冀甚宠惮之 - Means 'Sun Shou had a jealous and controlling nature, able to control Liang Ji, who greatly admired and feared her.' '性钳忌' means 'jealous and controlling nature,' '制御' means 'control,' and '宠惮' means 'admire and fear.'
  • 初，父商献美人友通期于顺帝 - Means 'Initially, her father, Shang, presented the beautiful woman You Tongqi to Emperor Shun.' '献' means 'presented,' and '美人' means 'beautiful woman.'
  • 通期有微过，帝以归商，商不敢留而出嫁之 - Means 'You Tongqi had a minor fault, so the emperor sent her back to Shang, who dared not keep her and instead married her off.' '微过' means 'minor fault,' and '出嫁之' means 'married her off.'
  • 冀即遣客盗还通期 - Means 'Liang Ji immediately sent someone to secretly bring You Tongqi back.' '遣客' means 'sent someone,' and '盗还' means 'secretly brought back.'
  • 会商薨，冀行服于城西，私与之居 - Means 'When Shang died, Liang Ji wore mourning clothes in the west of the city and secretly lived with You Tongqi.' '行服' means 'wore mourning clothes,' and '私与之居' means 'secretly lived with her.'
  • 寿伺冀出，多从苍头篡取通期归，截发刮面，答掠之，欲上书告其事 - Means 'Sun Shou waited for Liang Ji to leave, then took many servants and forcibly brought You Tongqi back, cutting her hair and beating her face, intending to report the matter to the emperor.' '伺' means 'waited,' '篡取' means 'forcibly brought back,' and '答掠' means 'beat.'
  • 冀大恐，顿首请于寿母 - Means 'Liang Ji was greatly terrified and knelt to beg for forgiveness from Sun Shou's mother.' '顿首' means 'knelt,' and '请' means 'beg.'
  • 寿亦不得已而止 - Means 'Sun Shou also had no choice but to stop.' '不得已' means 'had no choice.'
  • 冀嬖爱监奴秦宫，官至太仓令，得出入寿所 - Means 'Liang Ji favored and loved his eunuch Qin Gong, who was appointed as the chief granary officer and could enter and exit Sun Shou's residence.' '嬖爱' means 'favored and loved,' '监奴' means 'eunuch,' and '太仓令' means 'chief granary officer.'
  • 寿见宫辄屏御者，托以言事，因与私焉 - Means 'Whenever Sun Shou saw Qin Gong, she would send away her attendants, pretending to discuss matters, and then they would engage in private affairs.' '辄屏御者' means 'would send away her attendants,' '托以言事' means 'pretending to discuss matters,' and '因与私焉' means 'then they would engage in private affairs.'
  • 宫内外兼宠，威权大震，刺史二千石，皆谒辞之 - Means 'Qin Gong was favored both inside and outside the palace, wielding great power, and even officials like cishi and er qian shi would pay him homage.' '内外兼宠' means 'favored both inside and outside the palace,' '威权大震' means 'wielding great power,' and '谒辞之' means 'pay homage to him.'

  • 襄城君 - A noble title given to Sun Shou due to Liang Ji's favor.

  • 太仓令 - A high-ranking official responsible for the granary.
  • 刺史 - A regional governor.
  • 二千石 - A high-ranking official, equivalent to a modern-day minister.

  • 冀大起第舍 - Means 'Ji (a person's name) built large mansions.'
  • 对街为宅 - Means 'built houses facing each other across the street.'
  • 殚极土木 - Means 'exhausted all resources in earth and wood (construction materials).'
  • 互相夸竞 - Means 'competed with each other in ostentation.'
  • 堂寝皆有阴阳奥室连房洞户 - Means 'the halls and bedrooms all had rooms with hidden chambers, connected rooms, and open doors.'
  • 柱壁雕镂，加以铜漆 - Means 'the pillars and walls were carved and adorned with copper and lacquer.'
  • 窗牖皆有绮疏青琐，图以云气仙灵 - Means 'the windows and doors were decorated with intricate designs and painted with clouds, immortals, and divine beings.'
  • 台阁周通，更相临望 - Means 'the terraces and halls were interconnected, allowing mutual views.'
  • 飞梁石磴，陵跨水道 - Means 'bridges and stone steps soared over waterways.'
  • 金玉珠玑，异方珍怪，充积私室 - Means 'gold, jade, pearls, and exotic treasures filled their private rooms.'
  • 远致汗血名马 - Means 'brought distant famous horses known for their sweat and blood (a type of fine horse).'
  • 又广开园圃，采土筑山 - Means 'they also expanded gardens and used soil to build artificial mountains.'
  • 十里九坡，以象二崤 - Means 'nine slopes over ten miles, resembling the two passes (Xiao Passes).'
  • 深林绝渊，有若自然 - Means 'deep forests and precipitous gorges, as if naturally formed.'
  • 奇禽驯兽，飞走其间 - Means 'exotic birds and tamed animals flying and running among them.'
  • 冀、寿共乘辇车，张羽盖，饰以金银 - Means 'Ji and Shou (another person's name) rode in a carriage together, with a canopy covered in feathers and decorated with gold and silver.'
  • 游观第内，多从倡伎 - Means 'they toured their mansions, accompanied by many entertainers.'
  • 鸣钟吹管，酣讴竟路 - Means 'they sounded bells, blew pipes, and sang loudly all along the way.'
  • 或连继日夜，以骋娱恣 - Means 'sometimes they continued day and night to indulge in pleasure.'
  • 客到门不得通，皆请谢门者，门者累千金 - Means 'visitors could not enter, and had to bribe the gatekeepers, who accumulated thousands of gold coins.'
  • 后事败，皆自杀 - Means 'later, when their affairs failed, they both committed suicide.'
  • 财货，县官斥卖，合三十余万万，以充王府用 - Means 'their wealth was confiscated by the government and sold, totaling over thirty million, to replenish the imperial treasury.'
  • 减天下租税之半 - Means 'reducing the national taxes by half.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生于青州，小名齐奴 - Born in Qingzhou, his nickname was Qi Nu.

  • 独不及崇 - Exclusively not given to Shichong.
  • 此儿虽小，后能自立 - This child may be young now, but he will be able to stand on his own in the future.
  • 二十余为修武令，有能名 - In his twenties, he served as the magistrate of Xiubu and gained a reputation for ability.
  • 后伐吴有功，封安阳县侯 - Later, he was successful in the campaign against Wu and was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Anyang County.
  • 迁侍中，出为南中郎将，荆州刺史，领南蛮校尉，加鹰扬将军 - Promoted to the position of Grand Attendant, sent out to serve as the Southern Middle General, Governor of Jing Province, in charge of the Southern Barbarian Commandant, and appointed as the Flying General.
  • 崇在南中，得鸩鸟雏，以与后军将军王恺 - While Shichong was in the South, he obtained a young vulture and gave it to General Wang Kai of the rear army.
  • 时制，鸩鸟不得过江，为司隶校尉傅祗所纠 - At that time, according to the law, vultures were not allowed to cross the river, and he was reported by the Inspector of the Imperial Secretariat, Fu Zhi.
  • 诏原之，烧鸩于都街 - The emperor pardoned him, and the vulture was burned on the capital street.
  • 颖悟有才气，而任侠元行检 - Sharp-witted and talented, but he was a wanderer and lacked self-discipline.
  • 在荆州劫远使商客，致富不赀 - In Jing Province, he robbed foreign envoys and merchants, becoming immensely wealthy.
  • 征为大司农 - He was summoned to serve as the Grand Farmer.
  • 以征书未至，擅去官免 - Because the summons had not arrived, he resigned from his post and was dismissed.
  • 顷拜太仆，出为征虏将军，假节监徐州诸军事，镇下邳 - He was later appointed as the Grand Steward, sent out to serve as the General Who Subdues the Savages, entrusted with the military command of Xu Province, and garrisoned in Xiapi.
  • 崇有州馆在河阳之金谷，一名梓泽 - Shichong had a mansion in Jinyang's Jin Valley, also known as Zizi.
  • 送者倾都，帐饮于此焉 - All the people of the capital came to see him off, and they held a farewell banquet here.
  • 至镇，与徐州刺史高诞争酒相侮，为军司所奏免官 - Upon arrival, he quarreled with the Governor of Xu Province, Gao Dan, over drinking, and was impeached by the Military Commissioner and dismissed from his post.
  • 复拜卫尉，与潘岳谄事贾谧 - He was later appointed as the Grand Guardian and flattered Jia Mi along with Pan Yue.
  • 广城君每出，崇降车路左，望尘而拜 - Whenever Lady Guangcheng went out, Shichong would get off his carriage on the left side of the road and bow as she approached.

  • 崇后知之，因杀所告者 - Describes the final act of retribution.

  • 石氏之富 - Refers to the immense wealth of the Shi family, particularly Shi Chong, who was one of the richest men of his time.

  • 东方南方玉声轻洁，而性清凉 - This statement also reflects the belief in the qualities of jade from different regions, with Eastern and Southern jade being described as light and cool.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 使房内养之 is elliptical and can be interpreted as 'to have her raised in the inner quarters,' but it could also be read as 'to keep her in the inner quarters.'
  • 视如瓦石，聚如粪土 is a metaphorical expression that can be interpreted as treating the treasures as worthless, but it also emphasizes the vast quantity of wealth.
  • 殊方异国所得 is elliptical and can be interpreted as 'obtained from distant lands and foreign countries,' but it could also be read as 'obtained from exotic and foreign sources.'
  • 当指白日以汝为殉 is a euphemism that can be interpreted as a request for the woman to commit suicide, but it could also be read as a symbolic act of devotion.
  • 生爱死离，不如无爱 is a statement that can be interpreted as a philosophical reflection on the nature of love and death, but it could also be read as a pragmatic decision to avoid the pain of separation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亦以豪侈稱府中 - Also known in the government for extravagance. The phrase '豪侈' combines '豪' (brave, bold) and '侈' (extravagant), suggesting a bold and lavish lifestyle.
  • 人生但欢乐 - Life is just about enjoyment, emphasizing a hedonistic view of life.
  • 富贵在何时 - When will wealth and nobility come? Expresses a sense of uncertainty about future success.
  • 恣意酣赏 - Indulging in pleasure, describing a life of luxury and enjoyment.
  • 累从征讨 - Repeatedly followed military campaigns, indicating frequent military service.
  • 常为军锋 - Often led the vanguard, suggesting a leadership role in battles.
  • 历南谯泗竟陵太守 - Served as governor of Nanqiao, Si, and Jingling, listing his administrative positions.
  • 丈夫生如轻尘栖弱草 - A man's life is like a light dust settling on a weak blade of grass, a metaphor for the fragility and transience of life.
  • 白驹之过隙 - Like a white horse passing through a crevice, another metaphor for the fleeting nature of time.
  • 有眠床一张 - There is a bed, a simple description of an object.
  • 皆是蹙柏 - All made of compressed pine, describing the material of the bed.
  • 四面周匝 - Surrounded on all sides, indicating the bed's construction.
  • 无一有异 - None of which is different, suggesting uniformity.
  • 通用银镂金花寿福 - Commonly used silver inlaid with gold flowers and longevity symbols, describing the decorative elements of the bed.
  • 两重为脚 - With two layers as legs, describing the bed's structure.
  • 为湘东王镇西司马述职西上 - Served as the Western司马 of the Western Region for the Prince of Xiangdong, indicating a specific official position.
  • 道中乏食 - Lacked food on the road, describing a shortage of provisions.
  • 缘路采菱 - Collected lotus roots along the way, indicating a resourceful solution to the food shortage.
  • 作菱米饭给所部 - Made lotus root rice to feed his troops, describing the preparation of food.
  • 弘度之所过后，人觅一菱不得 - After Fish Hong passed by, no one could find a single lotus root, indicating the thoroughness of his collection.
  • 又于穷洲之上，捕得数百猕猴 - Also caught hundreds of monkeys on a remote island, describing another resourceful action.
  • 膳以为脯，以供酒食 - Made them into jerky to provide for feasts, indicating the use of the monkeys for food.
  • 比及江陵，资食复振 - By the time he reached Jiangling, his resources and food supply were restored, describing the recovery of his situation.
  • 逢敕迎瑞豫王，令送像下都 - Encountered an imperial order to welcome the Prince of Ruiyu and send him to the capital, indicating a new assignment.
  • 弘率部曲数百，悉衣锦袍 - Fish Hong led several hundred troops, all wearing brocade robes, describing his troops' attire.
  • 赫奕满道 - Shining brightly along the road, indicating a grand and impressive appearance.
  • 颇为人所慕 - Was greatly admired by people, indicating widespread admiration.

  • 纵恣不悛 - Indulging in excess without repentance, referring to unrestrained behavior.

  • 皆极天下之选 - All of the finest in the world, describing the selection of concubines.
  • 所幸姬江无畏 - His favored concubine Jiang Wuwei, indicating a specific person.
  • 服玩侔于齐东昏潘妃 - Her attire and ornaments were on par with those of Pan Fei of Qi, comparing her luxury to a historical figure.
  • 宝珥值千万 - Her jewels were worth millions, indicating immense wealth.
  • 好食鲭鱼头 - Loved eating the heads of mackerel, indicating a specific dietary preference.

  • 其他珍膳盈溢后房 - Other delicacies overflowing in the rear chambers, indicating abundance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 高阳王 - A title referring to a royal prince, specifically a member of the Northern Wei imperial family.
  • 居近青陽门外数里 - Literally 'resides near Qingyang Gate several miles away,' indicating the location of the prince's residence.
  • 御道西旁 - 'West of the imperial road,' specifying the exact position relative to the main thoroughfare.
  • 洛中之甲第 - 'The finest mansion in Luoyang,' where '甲第' means 'the best or most prestigious residence.'
  • 正光中雍为丞相 - 'During the Zhengguang era, Prince Yong served as Chancellor,' referring to a specific period and official position.
  • 给羽葆鼓吹虎贲班剑百人 - 'Provided with a retinue including feathered canopies, musical instruments, guards, and a hundred ceremonial swords,' describing the grandeur of the prince's entourage.
  • 贵极人臣，富兼山海 - 'His status was at the pinnacle of human officials, and his wealth was as vast as mountains and seas,' emphasizing the extreme power and riches.
  • 居第匹于帝宫 - 'His residence matched that of the imperial palace,' highlighting the opulence of the prince's home.
  • 白壁丹槛，窈窕连云 - 'White walls and red railings, elegant and reaching up to the clouds,' describing the architectural beauty.
  • 飞檐居宇，葛周通 - 'Overhanging eaves and interconnected halls,' further detailing the grand design of the mansion.
  • 童仆六千，伎女五百 - 'Six thousand servants and five hundred performing girls,' indicating the large household staff.
  • 隋珠照日，罗衣从凤 - 'Pearls like those of the State of Cui shining under the sun, silk robes adorned with phoenixes,' describing the luxurious attire.
  • 自汉晋以来，诸王豪侈，未之有也 - 'Since the Han and Jin dynasties, no prince has been as extravagant,' comparing the prince's wealth and luxury to previous eras.
  • 出则鸣驺御道，文物成行 - 'When he went out, there were heralds on the imperial road, and a procession of officials and treasures,' describing the grand procession.
  • 铙歌繁响，笳声哀怨 - 'The sound of bells and drums was loud, and the sound of pipes was mournful,' evoking the atmosphere of the procession.
  • 人则歌姬舞女，击筑吹笙，丝管迭奏，连宵尽日 - 'There were singing girls and dancing women, playing the zither and blowing the flute, with strings and pipes alternating, continuing through the night and day,' depicting the continuous entertainment.
  • 其竹林鱼池，佯于禁苑 - 'His bamboo groves and fish ponds were like those in the imperial gardens,' emphasizing the similarity to imperial landscapes.
  • 芳如积，珍木连阴 - 'Fragrant flowers were abundant, and precious trees cast long shadows,' describing the lush gardens.
  • 雍薨后，诸伎女悉令人道，或有出家者 - 'After Prince Yong's death, all the performing girls were sent to the Buddhist temples, some becoming nuns,' indicating the fate of the household after his death.
  • 美人徐月华善箜筷，能为明妃出塞之曲 - 'The beautiful lady Xu Yuehua was skilled in playing the konghou and could perform the tune of the Princess of Ming going out to the frontier,' highlighting her musical talent.
  • 闻者莫不动容 - 'Those who heard her were deeply moved,' emphasizing the emotional impact of her performance.
  • 永安中，与卫将军原士康为侧室 - 'During the Yong'an period, she became a concubine to the Marshal Yuan Shikang,' indicating her new role.
  • 士康宅亦近青阳门 - 'Marshal Yuan Shikang's residence was also near Qingyang Gate,' providing context for the location.
  • 徐鼓箜筷而歌，哀声人云 - 'Xu played the konghou and sang, her voice filled with sorrow,' describing her performance.
  • 行路听者，俄而成市 - 'Passersby gathered around her, forming a crowd,' indicating the popularity of her music.
  • 徐常语士康云 - 'Xu often told Marshal Yuan Shikang,' introducing a direct quote.
  • 王有二美姬，一名修容，二名艳姿 - 'The prince had two beautiful concubines, one named Xiurong and the other named Yanzī,' introducing the names of the concubines.
  • 蛾眉皓齿，洁貌倾城 - 'They had long, delicate eyebrows, white teeth, and a purity that could charm the entire city,' describing their physical beauty.
  • 修容亦为绿水歌，艳姿善逐风舞 - 'Xiurong could sing the *Lüshui* song, and Yanzī was skilled in the *Zhufeng* dance,' highlighting their talents.
  • 并爱倾后室，宠冠诸姬 - 'They both were beloved by the prince and were the most favored among all his concubines,' emphasizing their status.
  • 士康闻此，常令徐鼓绿水火凤之曲焉 - 'Upon hearing this, Marshal Yuan Shikang often asked Xu to play the *Lüshui Huafeng* song,' indicating the influence of the story.

  • 河間王 - A title indicating a king of the Hejian region.

  • 居山林之饒 - Literally 'residing in the abundance of mountains and forests,' indicating a luxurious lifestyle.
  • 爭修園宅 - Literally 'competing to build gardens and residences,' describing a rivalry in constructing grand estates.
  • 崇門豐室 - Literally 'high gates and spacious rooms,' indicating grand and luxurious architecture.
  • 阿戶連房 - Literally 'adjoining doors and connected rooms,' describing a large, interconnected living space.
  • 飛館生風 - Literally 'flying pavilions that generate wind,' suggesting elegant and airy structures.
  • 重樓起霧 - Literally 'tall buildings rising like mist,' indicating tall and impressive structures.
  • 高台芳榭 - Literally 'high platforms and fragrant pavilions,' describing beautiful and elevated structures.
  • 家家而筑 - Literally 'each household builds,' indicating widespread construction.
  • 花林曲池 - Literally 'flower forests and winding ponds,' describing lush and scenic gardens.
  • 園園而有 - Literally 'each garden has,' indicating that every garden features these elements.

  • 伎女三百人，盡皆國色 - Literally 'three hundred performers, all of them national beauties,' describing a large number of highly skilled and attractive performers.
  • 有婢朝雲，善吹，能作團扇歌、陇上聲 - Literally 'there was a servant named Chao Yun, skilled in playing the flute, able to play the 'Round Fan Song' and 'Song of the Mountains,' indicating a talented musician.
  • 秦州刺史 - The title of a governor of Qin Prefecture.
  • 諸羌外叛 - Literally 'the Qiang tribes rebelled,' indicating a rebellion by the Qiang people.
  • 琛令朝雲假為貧妪，吹而乞 - Literally 'King Chen ordered Chao Yun to pretend to be a poor old woman, playing the flute while begging,' describing a strategic use of music.
  • 何為棄坟井在山谷为寇也 - Literally 'Why do we abandon our homes and live as bandits in the mountains?' indicating a rhetorical question that leads to their surrender.
  • 秦民语曰 - Literally 'the people of Qin said,' indicating a quote from the local population.
  • 快马健儿，不如老瓯吹篪 - Literally 'swift horses and strong warriors are no match for an old woman blowing the flute,' emphasizing the power of music over military might.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無政績 - Means 'without political achievements' or 'ineffective in governance.'
  • 遣使向西域求名馬 - Means 'sent envoys to the Western Regions to seek famous horses.'
  • 遠至波斯國 - Means 'traveling as far as the Persian Empire.'
  • 得千里馬，號曰“追風赤” - Means 'obtained a thousand-mile horse, named it 'Chasing Wind Red.''
  • 其次有七百里者十余匹 - Means 'next were over ten horses that could run seven hundred miles.'
  • 以銀為槽，金為環鎖 - Means 'using silver for the manger and gold for the rings and locks.'
  • 諸王服其豪富 - Means 'the kings admired his wealth and opulence.'
  • 深嘗語人云 - Means 'he once told people.'
  • 晉室石崇乃是庶姓 - Means 'the Jin dynasty's Shi Chong was merely a commoner.'
  • 猶能雉頭狐腋，畫卯雕薪 - Means 'still he could afford peacock heads and fox furs, and carve ivory and carve wood.'
  • 況我大魏天王，不為華侈 - Means 'how much more so for the great Emperor of Wei, who should not be stingy with luxury.'
  • 造迎風館於後園 - Means 'built a Wind-Blocking Pavilion in the garden.'
  • 列錢青琐，玉鳳衔铃，金龍吐旆 - Means 'with silver coins on the green lattices, jade phoenixes holding bells, and golden dragons spewing banners.'
  • 秦柰朱李，株条人檐 - Means 'the branches of Qin plums and red pears extended into the eaves.'
  • 伎女楼上，坐而摘食 - Means 'dancers on the upper floor picked and ate the fruits while sitting.'
  • 陳諸寶器，金瓶、銀瓮百余口 - Means 'displayed various precious vessels, over a hundred gold bottles and silver jars.'
  • 水晶钵，瑪瑶琉璃碗，赤玉卮数十枚 - Means 'crystal bowls, jade and glass cups, and dozens of red jade cups.'
  • 工作奇妙，中土所无，皆从西来 - Means 'the craftsmanship was exquisite, not found in the Central Plains, all brought from the West.'
  • 陳女樂及諸名馬 - Means 'showed off the female entertainers and the famous horses.'
  • 案行府库，锦珠玑，冰罗雾，充积其内 - Means 'inspected the treasury, where silk, pearls, and fine fabrics were piled up.'
  • 琛謂章武王融曰 - Means 'Chen told Prince Zhangwu Rong.'
  • 不恨我不見石崇；恨石崇不見我 - Means 'I regret not seeing Shi Chong; I regret that Shi Chong did not see me.'
  • 融立性貪暴，志欲無限 - Means 'Rong had an insatiable greed and ambition.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 莫以今时宠，宁忘旧日恩 - Traditional Chinese poetic form; '莫以' means 'do not because,' '今时宠' refers to current favor, '宁' means 'how much more,' and '旧日恩' means 'former kindness.' Suggests not forgetting past kindness due to current favor.
  • 看花满目泪 - Literally 'seeing flowers with tears filling the eyes'; conveys deep emotional sorrow or nostalgia.
  • 不共楚王言 - 'Not sharing words with the King of Chu'; metaphorical way of saying the speaker feels isolated or misunderstood, as if even a king would not understand their feelings.
  • 元载未年 - 'In the later years of Yuan Zai'; '未年' means 'later years.'
  • 造芸辉堂于私第 - 'Built the Yunhui Hall in his private residence'; '造' means 'to build,' '芸辉' is a type of fragrant grass, '堂' means 'hall,' and '私第' means 'private residence.'
  • 芸辉，香草也，出于阗国 - 'Yunhui, a fragrant grass, comes from the country of Shatuo'; '香草' means 'fragrant grass' and '出于阗国' means 'comes from the country of Shatuo.'
  • 其香洁白如玉，入土不朽烂 - 'Its fragrance is pure white like jade, and it does not decay when buried in the ground'; describes the qualities of the fragrant grass.

  • 更构沉檀为梁栋 - 'Further constructing sandalwood as beams and pillars'; '更构' means 'further constructing,' '沉檀' means 'sandalwood,' and '梁栋' means 'beams and pillars.'
  • 饰金银为户牖 - 'Decorating with gold and silver for doors and windows'; '饰' means 'decorating,' '金银' means 'gold and silver,' and '户牖' means 'doors and windows.'
  • 内设悬黎屏风、紫绡帐 - 'Inside, there were set up a screen made of jade-like stone and a purple silk curtain'; '悬黎' means 'jade-like stone,' '屏风' means 'screen,' '紫绡帐' means 'purple silk curtain.'
  • 其屏风，本杨国忠之宝也 - 'The screen, originally a treasure of Yang Guozhong'; '本' means 'originally,' '杨国忠' means 'Yang Guozhong,' and '之宝' means 'treasure.'
  • 屏上刻前代美女伎乐之形 - 'On the screen, there were carvings of beautiful women and musical performances from previous generations'; '刻' means 'carvings,' '前代' means 'previous generations,' '美女' means 'beautiful women,' '伎乐' means 'musical performances,' and '之形' means 'forms.'
  • 外以玳瑁、水犀为押络 - 'Outside, it was adorned with tortoise shell and water buffalo horn'; '外' means 'outside,' '玳瑁' means 'tortoise shell,' '水犀' means 'water buffalo horn,' and '为押络' means 'adorned.'
  • 络以真珠瑟瑟 - 'Adorned with pearls and jade'; '络' means 'adorned,' '真珠' means 'pearls,' and '瑟瑟' means 'jade.'
  • 其为精妙，殆非人工所及 - 'Its craftsmanship is so exquisite that it seems beyond human skill'; '其为' means 'its,' '精妙' means 'exquisite,' '殆' means 'seems,' '非' means 'not,' '人工' means 'human skill,' and '所及' means 'beyond.'
  • 紫绢帐，得于南溪洞中之酋帅 - 'The purple silk curtain, obtained from the chieftain of the Nanchi Cave'; '得于' means 'obtained from,' '南溪洞中' means 'Nanchi Cave,' and '之酋帅' means 'chieftain.'
  • 轻疏而薄，如无所碍 - 'Light and thin, as if without obstruction'; '轻疏' means 'light and thin,' '而' means 'and,' '薄' means 'thin,' and '如无所碍' means 'as if without obstruction.'
  • 虽属凝冬，而风不能入 - 'Even in the depths of winter, the wind cannot enter'; '虽属' means 'even in,' '凝冬' means 'depths of winter,' '而' means 'but,' and '风不能入' means 'the wind cannot enter.'
  • 盛夏则清凉自至 - 'In the summer, coolness naturally arrives'; '盛夏' means 'summer,' '则' means 'then,' and '清凉自至' means 'coolness naturally arrives.'
  • 其色隐隐焉，忽不知其帐也 - 'Its color is faintly visible, and one suddenly does not notice it'; '其色' means 'its color,' '隐隐' means 'faintly visible,' '焉' is a particle, and '忽不知其帐也' means 'one suddenly does not notice it.'
  • 谓载卧内有紫气而服玩之 - 'It was said that there was a purple aura in Yuan Zai's sleeping chamber, and he enjoyed it'; '谓' means 'it was said,' '载卧内' means 'in Yuan Zai's sleeping chamber,' '有紫气' means 'there was a purple aura,' '而' means 'and,' '服玩之' means 'enjoyed it.'
  • 奢僭拟于帝王之家 - 'His extravagance was comparable to that of a royal family'; '奢僭' means 'extravagance,' '拟于' means 'comparable to,' and '帝王之家' means 'royal family.'
  • 芸辉之前有池，悉以白石砌其岸 - 'In front of Yunhui, there was a pond, and the banks were entirely lined with white stones'; '芸辉之前' means 'in front of Yunhui,' '有池' means 'there was a pond,' '悉' means 'entirely,' '以白石' means 'with white stones,' and '砌其岸' means 'lined the banks.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '今诚复通，为置郡县，愈于南夷' is a conditional statement, suggesting a comparison between establishing counties and dealing with the southern barbarians.
  • The sentence '乃拜相如为中郎将，建节往使' is a sequence of actions, with the appointment and mission being closely linked.
  • The sentence '副使王然于壶充国，吕越人驰四乘之传' lists the names of the deputy envoys and describes their mode of travel.
  • The sentence '因巴蜀吏市物以赂西夷' describes the method of acquiring goods for the purpose of bribery.
  • The sentence '县令负弩矢先驱' describes the role of the county magistrate in the procession.
  • The sentence '卓王孙喟然而叹，自以得使女尚司马长卿晚' describes Zhuo Wangsun's reaction and his feelings about marrying his daughter to Sima Changqing.
  • The sentence '厚分与其女财与男等同' describes the distribution of wealth to his daughter and sons.
  • The sentence '便略定西夷' describes the quick pacification of the western barbarians.
  • The sentence '邛冉斯榆之君，皆请为内臣' describes the submission of the kings of Qiong, Ran, Si, and Yu.
  • The sentence '除边关，关益斥，西至沫、若水，南至为徼' describes the extension of the borders.
  • The sentence '通零关道，桥孙水以通邛都' describes the opening of the Lingguan Road and the construction of a bridge.
  • The sentence '还报天子，天子大悦' describes the emperor's reaction to the report.
  • The sentence '其后，有人上书言相如使时受金，失官' describes the accusation against Xiangru and the loss of his position.

  • 抱颈而泣 - 抱颈 means 'embracing the neck,' 泣 means 'to cry.' This phrase describes a gesture of deep emotion, possibly a way of expressing affection or distress.
  • 平生富足 - 平生 means 'throughout one's life,' 富足 means 'wealthy' or 'well-off.' This phrase indicates that she was wealthy in her previous life.
  • 以衣裘贳酒 - 以 means 'to use,' 衣裘 means 'clothing and fur,' 贳酒 means 'to borrow wine.' This phrase describes the act of using their clothes and fur as collateral to borrow wine.
  • 于成都卖酒 - 于 means 'in,' 成都 is the city, 卖酒 means 'to sell wine.' This phrase indicates that they started selling wine in Chengdu.
  • 亲著犊鼻涤器 - 亲著 means 'to personally wear,' 犊鼻 refers to a type of clothing (often a short garment), 涤器 means 'to wash dishes.' This phrase describes how Sima Xiangru personally wore a short garment to wash dishes.
  • 耻王孙 - 耻 means 'to shame,' 王孙 refers to Wang Jun, the son of the governor. This phrase indicates that Sima Xiangru's actions were meant to shame Wang Jun.
  • 果以为病 - 果 means 'indeed' or 'really,' 以 means 'to consider,' 病 means 'a problem' or 'an issue.' This phrase indicates that Wang Jun indeed considered this a problem.
  • 厚给文君 - 厚给 means 'to provide generously,' 文君 is the name of Zhuo Wenjun. This phrase indicates that Wang Jun provided her with generous support.
  • 姣好 - Means 'beautiful' or 'handsome.'
  • 眉色如望远山 - 眉色 means 'eyebrow color,' 如 means 'like,' 望远山 means 'looking at distant mountains.' This phrase describes her eyebrows as resembling distant mountains.
  • 脸际常若芙蓉 - 脸际 means 'face,' 常 means 'always,' 若 means 'like,' 芙蓉 means 'lotus flower.' This phrase describes her face as always looking like a lotus flower.
  • 肌肤柔滑如脂 - 肌肤 means 'skin,' 柔滑 means 'soft and smooth,' 如脂 means 'like fat.' This phrase describes her skin as soft and smooth like fat.
  • 十七而寡 - 十七 means 'seventeen,' 而寡 means 'became a widow.' This phrase indicates that she became a widow at the age of seventeen.
  • 为人放诞风流 - 为人 means 'to be,' 放诞 means 'free-spirited' or 'reckless,' 风流 means 'charming' or 'romantic.' This phrase describes her as free-spirited and charming.
  • 悦长卿之才而越礼焉 - 悦 means 'to be pleased,' 长卿 is the courtesy name of Sima Xiangru, 之才 means 'his talent,' 越礼 means 'to violate propriety.' This phrase indicates that she was pleased with his talent and violated propriety by doing so.
  • 素有消渴疾 - 素 means 'always' or 'originally,' 有 means 'to have,' 消渴疾 means 'consumptive disease' (a term for diabetes). This phrase indicates that he originally had a consumptive disease.
  • 悦文君之色 - 悦 means 'to be pleased,' 文君 is the name of Zhuo Wenjun, 之色 means 'her beauty.' This phrase indicates that he was pleased by her beauty.
  • 遂以发痼疾 - 遂 means 'then' or 'therefore,' 以 means 'to cause,' 发痼疾 means 'to trigger a chronic illness.' This phrase indicates that he triggered a chronic illness as a result.
  • 乃作美人赋 - 乃 means 'then' or 'therefore,' 作 means 'to write,' 美人赋 means 'Ode to a Beautiful Woman.' This phrase indicates that he then wrote an ode to a beautiful woman.
  • 欲以自刺 - 欲 means 'to want,' 以 means 'to use,' 自刺 means 'to self-criticize' or 'to self-reproach.' This phrase indicates that he wanted to use it to criticize himself.
  • 而终不能改 - 而 means 'but,' 终 means 'finally,' 不能改 means 'could not change.' This phrase indicates that he could not change his behavior.
  • 卒以此疾至死 - 卒 means 'finally' or 'ultimately,' 以 means 'because of,' 此疾 means 'this illness,' 至死 means 'to death.' This phrase indicates that he ultimately died because of this illness.
  • 为诔传于世 - 为 means 'to write,' 诔 means 'an elegy,' 传于世 means 'to pass down to posterity.' This phrase indicates that she wrote an elegy that was passed down to posterity.

  • 是夕岁二月旬有四日矣 - Specifies the date as the fourteenth day of the second month, providing a precise temporal context.
  • 崔之东有杏花一树，扳援可逾 - Describes a tree of apricot blossoms east of Cui's residence, which can be climbed over, suggesting a physical obstacle that Zhang overcomes.
  • 既望之夕，张因梯其树而逾焉 - Means 'On the night of the full moon, Zhang climbed the tree and jumped over it,' describing Zhang's actions to reach the western厢房.
  • 达于西厢，则户半开矣 - Means 'He arrived at the western厢房, and the door was half-open,' continuing the description of Zhang's arrival.
  • 红娘寝于床上，因惊之 - Means 'Hongniang was sleeping in bed, and Zhang startled her,' indicating the unexpected encounter.
  • 连曰：“至矣，至矣！” - Means 'She repeatedly said, 'Here she comes, here she comes!'' showing Hongniang's excitement and anticipation.
  • 张生且喜且骇，必谓获济 - Means 'Zhang was both happy and startled, believing he would succeed,' expressing Zhang's emotional state and hopes.
  • 及女至，则端服严容，大数张曰 - Means 'When the girl arrived, she was dressed formally and sternly, severely reprimanding Zhang,' describing the girl's reaction and the tone of her rebuke.
  • 兄之恩，活我之家厚矣 - Means 'Your kindness has greatly helped my family,' acknowledging Zhang's previous assistance.
  • 是以慈母以弱子幼女见托 - Means 'Therefore, my mother entrusted me with my young son and daughter,' explaining the trust placed in Zhang.
  • 奈何因不令之婢，致淫逸之词 - Means 'How could you, through an unseemly servant, cause such lewd words?' criticizing Zhang for the inappropriate behavior.
  • 始以护人之乱为义，而终掠乱以求之 - Means 'Initially, you claimed it was right to protect someone in chaos, but ultimately, you sought to take advantage of it,' highlighting the inconsistency in Zhang's actions.
  • 是以乱易乱，其去几何 - Means 'Thus, replacing one chaos with another, how far apart are they?' questioning the validity of Zhang's actions.
  • 诚欲寝其词，则保人之好，不义 - Means 'If you truly wanted to suppress these words, it would be unjust to protect someone's reputation,' arguing against concealing the truth.
  • 明之于母，则背人之惠，不祥 - Means 'If you revealed it to my mother, it would be unlucky to betray someone's kindness,' warning against revealing the truth.
  • 将寄于婢仆，又惧不得发其真诚 - Means 'If you entrusted it to servants, you would fear they might not convey your true intentions,' expressing concern about the reliability of servants.
  • 是用托短章，愿自陈启 - Means 'Therefore, I entrust this short poem, hoping to explain myself,' indicating the purpose of the poem.
  • 犹惧兄之见难，是用鄙靡之词，以求其必至 - Means 'Yet, I still fear you might find it difficult, so I use humble and flowery words to ensure you come,' expressing the girl's hope and the method she uses.
  • 非礼之动，能不愧心 - Means 'Such improper actions, how can one not feel ashamed?' questioning the morality of the actions.
  • 特愿以礼自持，无及于乱 - Means 'I only wish to maintain propriety and avoid chaos,' expressing the girl's desire for proper conduct.
  • 言毕，翻然而逝 - Means 'After finishing her words, she quickly left,' describing the girl's departure.
  • 张自失者久之，复逾而出，于是绝望 - Means 'Zhang was lost in thought for a long time, then climbed back out, and thus lost all hope,' describing Zhang's emotional state and actions after the encounter.

  • 待张之意甚厚 - She treats Zhang with great kindness; '厚' means 'kind' or 'generous.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Is after ten or more days, disappeared without a trace.
  • Hongniang's arrival, the act of giving the poem, and the purpose of giving it to Cui.
  • A pattern of behavior: she is allowed in again, leaves in the morning and returns in the evening.
  • Repeated meetings over a period of time at the West Chamber.
  • A dialogue: Zhang Sheng often questioned the situation of Zheng, and she said, 'I know it is impossible to do anything about it, so I wanted to make it happen.'
  • Zhang Sheng's departure, the act of writing a poem, and the purpose of persuasion.
  • Cui's response and her emotional state: she had no difficult words, but her sorrowful expression moved people.
  • The night before he left, he was no longer seen.
  • Zhang Sheng often wrote poems to flirt with her, but she rarely responded.
  • In general, what makes Cui stand out: she is determined to go all the way, but appears as if she didn't know; her words are quick and eloquent, but she rarely responds; she treats Zhang with great kindness, but she never followed up with words.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening simile '凡为文章，犹乘骐骥' governs the whole sentence and frames writing through the logic of horsemanship.
  • The concessive pattern '虽有逸气，当以衔勒制之' shows that natural brilliance is acknowledged but must still be restrained.
  • The final prohibition '勿使流乱轨躅，放意填坑岸也' can be read as two linked dangers: straying from the proper track and letting the writing run off into structural disorder.

  • 幽恨尚沉结 - '幽恨' means 'deep-seated sorrow' or 'hidden resentment,' and '沉结' means 'accumulated' or 'entrenched.' Together, they convey a sense of unresolved, lingering sorrow.
  • 哪堪一年事，长遣一宵说 - '哪堪' means 'how can one bear' or 'how can one endure,' and '长遣一宵说' means 'to entrust an entire year's worth of events to be told in one night.' This phrase expresses the difficulty of compressing a year's worth of experiences into a single night's conversation.
  • 但感久相思，何暇暂相悦 - '但感' means 'only feel,' '久相思' means 'longing for a long time,' and '何暇暂相悦' means 'how can there be time for a brief moment of pleasure.' This conveys the idea that the speaker is too consumed by longing to enjoy even a moment of happiness.
  • 虹桥薄夜成，龙驾寝晨列 - '虹桥' refers to a rainbow-like bridge, and '薄夜成' means 'formed at dusk.' '龙驾寝晨列' means 'the dragon carriage is arranged at dawn.' This likely refers to a celestial or mythological scene.
  • 生憎野鹊性迟回，死恨天鸡识时节 - '生憎' means 'hate in life,' and '野鹊性迟回' refers to a wild magpie's slow or hesitant nature. '死恨' means 'hate in death,' and '天鸡识时节' refers to a mythical rooster that knows the time. This contrasts the magpie's tardiness with the rooster's punctuality.
  • 曙色渐瞳，华星次明灭 - '曙色渐瞳' means 'the dawn light gradually brightens,' and '华星次明灭' means 'the stars shine and fade in succession.' This describes the transition from night to day.
  • 有此迢递期，不如死生别 - '迢递期' means 'a distant and long-awaited meeting,' and '不如死生别' means 'it would be better to part ways in life and death.' This expresses the speaker's frustration with the prolonged separation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虹桥 - Refers to a rainbow-like bridge, which in Chinese mythology is often associated with the connection between heaven and earth.
  • 龙驾 - Refers to the carriage of a dragon, which is a symbol of imperial power and celestial authority.
  • 野鹊 - Refers to a wild magpie, which in Chinese culture is often associated with tardiness or hesitation.
  • 天鸡 - Refers to a mythical rooster that is said to announce the dawn, symbolizing punctuality and the passage of time.
  • 曙色 - Refers to the dawn light, a common motif in Chinese poetry and literature symbolizing the end of night and the beginning of a new day.
  • 华星 - Refers to bright stars, often used in Chinese literature to symbolize beauty or the passage of time.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '幽恨尚沉结' is somewhat ambiguous in terms of its exact emotional weight, but it clearly conveys a sense of unresolved sorrow.
  • The phrase '长遣一宵说' is elliptical and could be interpreted as either 'to entrust an entire year's worth of events to be told in one night' or 'to recount an entire year's worth of events in one night.'
  • The contrast between the magpie's tardiness and the rooster's punctuality is clear, but the exact emotional tone of the speaker's frustration is somewhat ambiguous.
  • The phrase '不如死生别' is a strong expression of frustration, but the exact emotional weight of the speaker's desire for separation is somewhat ambiguous.
  • The phrase '天公隔是妒相怜' is somewhat ambiguous in terms of its exact emotional weight, but it clearly conveys a sense of frustration with the heavens.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 画梁 - In Chinese architecture, a '画梁' (painted beam) is a decorative element often found in traditional buildings, particularly in temples and palaces.

  • 夜夜相思 - The concept of '相思' (yearning) is a common theme in Chinese literature, often associated with romantic love and longing.
  • 飞蝶梦 - Dreams involving butterflies are often used in Chinese literature to symbolize the transient nature of life and the fleeting nature of desires.
  • 东风着意 - The '东风' (east wind) is often associated with spring and renewal in Chinese poetry, symbolizing the gentle and nurturing aspects of nature.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The line '画梁双燕舞娇尘' describes a scene where two swallows are dancing amidst the delicate dust on a painted beam, evoking a sense of grace and beauty.
  • The line '只见新诗不见人' suggests that the only thing visible is the new poetry, implying that the person who wrote the poetry is absent or hidden.
  • The line '夜夜相思飞蝶梦' expresses the nightly longing and the dream of flying butterflies, suggesting a deep emotional attachment and the fleeting nature of desires.
  • The line '东风着意杏花春' describes the east wind showing favor to the blooming apricot flowers in spring, symbolizing renewal and the arrival of spring.

  • 色可得而有兮，才孰俪而孤芳；不可得而见兮，心殷殷而愈彰 - References to the beauty and talent of a person, often used to describe the depth of a person's presence.
  • 迨夫母梦之初觉，余亦揽涕而成章 - References to the moment of waking from a dream and writing a poem, often used to describe the expression of deep emotions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 昆山玉树，阆苑琼葩，岂人间之凡植 - This opening triplet sets up the subject as extraordinary, but the syntax can be read as a question about its origin.
  • 储芳而艳，吐日春华 - This phrase can be read as a description of the subject's qualities, but the syntax can also be interpreted as a metaphor for its enduring beauty.
  • 祥云为盖，皓月为家 - This metaphorical phrase can be read as a description of the subject's ideal existence, but the syntax can also be interpreted as a question about its shelter.
  • 俄惊骖于怪雨，痤遗彩于尘沙 - This phrase can be read as a description of the subject's misfortune, but the syntax can also be interpreted as a question about its suddenness.
  • 啼玉弯而自惜，愁翠风而空嗟 - This metaphorical phrase can be read as a description of the subject's sorrow, but the syntax can also be interpreted as a question about its depth.
  • 玉容如在，瑶佩何之 - This phrase can be read as a description of the subject's beauty, but the syntax can also be interpreted as a question about its loss.
  • 生也何待，死也何为 - This philosophical question can be read as a reflection on the purpose of life and death.

  • 伤心春与花俱尽 - Means 'the sorrow of spring and flowers fading together,' a metaphor for the fleeting nature of beauty and youth.

  • 死生俱梦幻 - Means 'death and life are both like dreams,' suggesting the ephemeral nature of existence.
  • 来往只诗篇 - Means 'the only thing that remains are the poems,' emphasizing the enduring power of literature.
  • 玉佩惊沉水 - Means 'the jade pendant is startled by the sinking water,' a metaphor for the sudden loss of something precious.
  • 瑶琴怆断弦 - Means 'the jade zither's strings are broken in sorrow,' symbolizing the emotional pain of loss.
  • 伤心数行泪，尽日落花前 - Means 'tears fall in sorrow, day after day, in front of falling flowers,' a poignant image of continuous mourning.
  • 余召箕仙众，留得一词云 - Means 'I summoned the spirits of the箕 (a type of bamboo) and they left a poem behind,' indicating a mystical or supernatural element.
  • 绿惨双驾，香魂犹自多迷恋 - Means 'the green and sad double carriage, the fragrant soul still deeply attached,' a metaphor for a lingering attachment to the past.
  • 芳心密语在身边，如见诗人面 - Means 'the heart of the flower, intimate words by the side, as if seeing the face of the poet,' suggesting a deep emotional connection.
  • 又是柔肠未断，奈天不从人愿 - Means 'again, the tender heart is not broken, but heaven does not follow human wishes,' expressing the frustration of unfulfilled desires.
  • 琼销玉减，梦魂空有几多愁怨 - Means 'the jade and jade-like things are reduced, the dream soul is empty with many sorrows and grievances,' a metaphor for the fading of beauty and the persistence of sorrow.
  • 任东风老去吹不断，泪盈盈 - Means 'let the east wind blow away the old age, but the tears keep flowing,' a metaphor for the persistence of sorrow despite the passage of time.
  • 记春浅春深，春寒春暖，春雨春晴，都来杀诗人兴 - Means 'remembering the shallow and deep spring, the cold and warm spring, the rainy and sunny spring, all of which kill the poet's inspiration,' a metaphor for the various moods of spring affecting the poet's creativity.
  • 更落花元定，挽春情 - Means 'more falling flowers, originally determined to hold onto spring feelings,' a metaphor for the persistence of love despite the fading of spring.
  • 芳草犹迷舞蝶，绿杨空晤流鸳 - Means 'fragrant grass still confuses dancing butterflies, green willows vainly meet flowing mandarin ducks,' a metaphor for the confusion and futility of love.
  • 玄霜着意初成，回首失云英 - Means 'the purple frost is intentionally formed, looking back and losing the cloud and jade,' a metaphor for the loss of beauty and purity.
  • 但如醉如疾，如狂如舞，如梦如惊 - Means 'but like being drunk, like being sick, like being crazy, like dancing, like being in a dream, like being startled,' a series of metaphors for intense emotional states.
  • 香魂至今迷恋，问真仙消息最分明 - Means 'the fragrant soul is still deeply attached, asking the true immortal for the clearest message,' a metaphor for the persistence of love and the search for answers.
  • 后夜相逢何处，清风明月蓬瀛 - Means 'where will we meet again in the future, in the clear wind and bright moon of the fairyland,' a metaphor for the hope of a future reunion in a beautiful, ideal place.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 余尝过灵之家 - I once visited Ling's home, where '尝' (cháng) indicates a past experience, and '过' (guò) means 'to visit.'
  • 但见门掩夕晖兮 - Only saw the door closed under the evening light, where '但见' (dàn jiàn) means 'only see,' and '掩' (yǎn) means 'closed' or 'shut.'
  • 草沿阶而春色怜人 - Grass along the steps, the spring scenery touches one's heart, where '怜人' (lián rén) means 'to touch one's heart' or 'to move one.'
  • 疑为我之来兮 - Doubting that it was for my coming, where '疑' (yí) means 'doubt,' and '为' (wèi) means 'for.'
  • 空仿佛乎灵之魂独在也 - Empty, as if Ling's soul alone remained, where '空仿佛' (kōng fǎngfú) means 'empty, as if,' and '独在' (dú zài) means 'alone remains.'
  • 吾谓灵飘霞佩于太清兮 - I say Ling wears a brocade belt in the vast sky, where '谓' (wèi) means 'say,' and '太清' (tài qīng) refers to the highest heavens.
  • 拟群仙于瑶池 - Compared to the immortals in the Jade Pool, where '拟' (nǐ) means 'compare,' and '瑶池' (yáo chí) is the mythical pool of the immortals.
  • 透迄而不忍去兮 - Penetrating through and unable to leave, where '透迄' (tòu qì) means 'penetrating through,' and '不忍去' (bù rěn qù) means 'unable to leave.'
  • 欲与余而追随 - Wishing to follow me, where '欲' (yù) means 'wish,' and '追随' (zhuī suí) means 'follow.'
  • 余固知灵之同心兮 - I firmly know Ling shares my heart, where '固' (gù) means 'firmly,' and '同心' (tóng xīn) means 'share the same heart.'
  • 虽同往而何辞 - Even if we go together, why refuse? where '虽' (suī) means 'even if,' and '何辞' (hé cí) means 'why refuse.'
  • 忽返睨乎故乡兮 - Suddenly looking back at my hometown, where '返睨' (fǎn nì) means 'look back,' and '故乡' (gù xiāng) means 'hometown.'
  • 念众雏之无依 - Thinking of the young ones without support, where '念' (niàn) means 'think,' and '无依' (wú yī) means 'without support.'
  • 灵书勉今以自爱兮 - Ling wrote to encourage self-love today, where '书' (shū) means 'write,' and '勉' (miǎn) means 'encourage.'
  • 何既死而忽遗 - Why, after death, suddenly leave behind? where '既死' (jì sǐ) means 'after death,' and '忽遗' (hū yí) means 'suddenly leave behind.'
  • 翳母氏之念而死兮 - Shrouded in the mother's thoughts of death, where '翳' (yì) means 'shrouded,' and '念而死' (niàn ér sǐ) means 'thoughts of death.'
  • 谅虽悔而曷追 - Surely, even if regretful, how can it be pursued? where '谅' (liàng) means 'surely,' and '曷追' (hé zhuī) means 'how can it be pursued.'
  • 余于义未可以死兮 - I, in terms of righteousness, have not yet reached the point of death, where '于义' (yú yì) means 'in terms of righteousness,' and '未可以死' (wèi kě yǐ sǐ) means 'have not yet reached the point of death.'
  • 则亦付修短之有期 - Then it is also left to the natural course of life, where '付修短' (fù xiū duǎn) means 'left to the natural course,' and '之有期' (zhī yǒu qī) means 'with a set time.'
  • 畴昔之夜 - On that night long ago, where '畴昔' (chóuxī) means 'long ago.'
  • 忽有推余髻而泣者 - Suddenly, someone pushed my hair and wept, where '推' (tuī) means 'push,' and '髻' (jì) means 'hair.'
  • 非灵也那 - Not Ling, then? where '非' (fēi) means 'not,' and '那' (nà) is an interrogative particle.
  • 恍一梦之惊觉 - Suddenly awakening from a dream, where '恍' (huǎng) means 'suddenly,' and '惊觉' (jīng jué) means 'awakening.'
  • 空伏枕之涟漪 - Empty, the pillow is wet with tears, where '空' (kōng) means 'empty,' and '涟漪' (liányī) means 'ripples' or 'tears.'
  • 怆余怀之郁结 - My heart is filled with sorrow and bitterness, where '怆' (chuàng) means 'sorrow,' and '郁结' (yù jié) means 'bitterness.'
  • 重抑愤之哀词 - Repeatedly suppressing anger and writing mournful words, where '重抑' (zhòng yì) means 'repeatedly suppress,' and '哀词' (āi cí) means 'mournful words.'
  • 母知天知，有知无知，吾独自知耳 - Mother knows, heaven knows, those who know and those who don't know, I alone know, where '母知天知' (mǔ zhī tiān zhī) means 'Mother knows, heaven knows,' and '吾独自知耳' (wú dú zì zhī ěr) means 'I alone know.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 自爱 - A concept emphasizing self-love and self-care, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 修短之有期 - Refers to the natural course of life, with a set time for birth and death, a common theme in classical Chinese philosophy.

  • 姑苏台上月团团，姑苏台下水潺潺 - Poetic description of the moon over and water under the Terrace of Ku-su (Gusu), evoking a serene and picturesque scene.

  • 香魂玉骨归何处，不及真娘葬虎丘 - Poignant question about the fate of Xi Shi's spirit and body, contrasting her with the burial of Zhen Niang at Tiger Hill, suggesting a difference in their legacies.

  • 寒山寺里钟声早，渔火江风恼客眠 - Description of the early bell sounds from Hanshan Temple and the disturbance caused by fishing lights and river winds to travelers' sleep, highlighting the impact of natural and man-made sounds on human emotions.
  • 洞庭余柑三寸黄，笠泽银鱼一尺长 - Depiction of the small, yellow citrus fruit from Dongting Lake and the long, silver fish from Lake Lize, showcasing the local delicacies and their unique characteristics.

  • 早起腥风满城市，郎从海口贩鲜回 - Scene of the morning air filled with the smell of fish, suggesting that the husband has returned from the coast with fresh catches, highlighting the daily life and economy of the coastal region.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姑苏台 - The Terrace of Ku-su (Gusu), an ancient structure in Suzhou, known for its historical and cultural significance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 顾生年少标致，门户亦正相敌 - Reference to the importance of social status and family background in arranged marriages, emphasizing the match between the two families.
  • 吴下人多知之，或传为掌记云 - Reference to the region of Wu (modern-day Jiangsu and Zhejiang) and the tradition of recording and sharing local stories, often as a form of social commentary or entertainment.

  • 三哥 - The term of address for the third brother.
  • 百一姐 - The term of endearment or specific name for the hundred-first sister.

  • 未尝敢妄一邪言相及 - Means never daring to speak a single inappropriate word, highlighting the restraint and respect shown.
  • 察其动静 - Means observing her actions and reactions, indicating careful attention.

  • 赋情特甚也 - Means having a particularly strong emotional nature, emphasizing the intensity of her feelings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庭院深沉 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the courtyard is deep and secluded,' often used to evoke a sense of seclusion or privacy.
  • 遲遲日上荼架 - Poetic expression describing the sun slowly rising over the tea plant (荼) trellis, suggesting a slow and peaceful morning.
  • 芳丛相亚 - Vivid description of fragrant flowers arranged in a graceful manner, indicating beauty and elegance.
  • 裝點春无价 - Means 'adorned with the priceless beauty of spring,' emphasizing the value and beauty of the spring scenery.
  • 玉体香肌 - Poetic description of someone's body, suggesting it is as beautiful and delicate as jade and fragrant as musk.
  • 好手应难画 - Means 'even a skilled hand would find it difficult to paint,' indicating the difficulty in capturing the beauty of the scene.
  • 还惊讶 - Means 'still surprised,' expressing a sense of astonishment.
  • 春心荡也 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the heart is stirred by the spring,' suggesting emotional turmoil or excitement.
  • 谁共游蜂话 - Means 'who will share in the conversation with the bees,' implying a desire for companionship or interaction with nature.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庭院深沉 - Commonly used in classical literature to describe a secluded and peaceful environment.
  • 遲遲日上荼架 - Tea plants (荼) are often associated with traditional Chinese gardens and are symbolic of tranquility and elegance.
  • 芳丛相亚 - Fragrant flowers are often used in classical poetry to symbolize beauty and grace.
  • 裝點春无价 - The concept of spring being priceless is a common theme in classical literature, emphasizing the transient and precious nature of spring.
  • 玉体香肌 - This phrase is often used to describe the beauty and delicacy of a woman, reflecting traditional Chinese aesthetics.
  • 好手应难画 - The idea of even a skilled artist finding it difficult to capture the beauty of a scene is a common theme in classical literature.
  • 还惊讶 - Surprised by the beauty or the unexpected, a common emotion in classical literature.
  • 春心荡也 - The emotional response to the beauty of spring, often depicted as a mix of joy and melancholy.
  • 谁共游蜂话 - The desire for companionship or interaction with nature, a common theme in classical poetry.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening couplet sets the scene and mood, describing a deep and secluded courtyard with the sun slowly rising over the tea plant trellis.
  • This couplet describes the fragrant flowers arranged in a graceful manner, adorning the priceless beauty of spring.
  • This couplet describes the beauty and delicacy of someone's body, suggesting that even a skilled artist would find it difficult to capture such beauty.
  • This final couplet expresses a sense of astonishment, the emotional turmoil stirred by the spring, and a desire for companionship or interaction with nature.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 语未毕 - Means 'before the words were finished' or 'while the speech was still ongoing.'
  • 妗因素水涤觞 - The phrase is somewhat ambiguous. '妗' likely refers to a relative or a woman of high status, '因素水' suggests using a specific type of water, and '涤觞' means 'to wash the cups.' The phrase could be interpreted as 'the aunt washed the cups with a particular type of water.'
  • 娇乃引去 - Means 'the maiden then withdrew' or 'the maiden left.'
  • 自此，生复留意 - Means 'from this point on, the young man began to pay attention again.'
  • 一夕，娇独坐于堂恻借花轩内 - Means 'one evening, the maiden sat alone in the flower-borrowing pavilion beside the hall.'
  • 生偶至座侧 - Means 'the young man happened to come to her side.'
  • 见娇凭栏无语，徙倚沉吟 - Means 'he saw the maiden leaning against the railing, silent and pacing thoughtfully.'
  • 时花槛中有牡丹数本，欲开未开 - Means 'at that time, there were several peony plants in the flower trellis, about to bloom but not yet fully open.'
  • 生因为二绝以戏之曰 - Means 'the young man composed two short poems to tease her, saying...'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妗 - Likely refers to a relative, possibly an aunt, but the exact relationship is not specified.
  • 娇 - Refers to a maiden or young woman, often used in classical literature to describe a beautiful and graceful woman.
  • 堂恻借花轩 - '堂恻' means 'beside the hall,' and '借花轩' is a pavilion where flowers are borrowed or admired, often a place of beauty and contemplation.
  • 牡丹 - Peony, a symbol of wealth, honor, and beauty in Chinese culture.
  • 二绝 - Refers to two short poems, often used in classical Chinese literature for concise and elegant expressions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 妗因素水涤觞，娇乃引去 - This phrase is somewhat elliptical. It could be interpreted as 'the aunt washed the cups with a particular type of water, and the maiden then withdrew.'
  • 自此，生复留意 - This phrase marks a shift in the young man's attention, but the exact timing and context are not fully clear.
  • 一夕，娇独坐于堂恻借花轩内，生偶至座侧，见娇凭栏无语，徙倚沉吟 - This sequence of events is compact and must be read as a continuous narrative, with the young man's arrival and the maiden's silence forming a poignant moment.
  • 时花槛中有牡丹数本，欲开未开 - This description sets the scene and adds a layer of symbolism, as the peonies are about to bloom but not yet fully open, mirroring the maiden's state of mind.
